learn.title: Meet was / were
learn.html:
was and were are the past of the verb be. They tell us how things were before — where you were, how you felt, what something was like.

If am / is / are is the present, then was / were is simply its past twin.

🎯
Use them with past time words: yesterday, last night, in 2010, two hours ago.

learn.title: When do we use it?
learn.html:
📍 Past place
I was at home yesterday.
😊 Past feeling
They were tired after the trip.
🌤️ Past description
The weather was lovely.
👶 Age / time
She was born in May.
yesterday
●
now

The state is finished — it belongs to the past.

learn.title: Structure — two words only
learn.html:
I / he / she / it+was
you / we / they+were

Negative, question & short answers

+He was late.
−He wasn't late.
?Was he late?
short answerYes, he was. / No, he wasn't.

For you/we/they: weren't and Were …?

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Where were you last night?
I was at the cinema. The film was great!
Were your friends there too?
Yes, they were. We were all together.
🏖️ Holiday
"The beach was beautiful and the people were friendly."
🎂 Childhood
"When I was six, we were very happy."

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
You was late.  ✓ You were late.
✗
They was happy.  ✓ They were happy.
✗
Was you at home?  ✓ Were you at home?
✗
I didn't was there.  ✓ I wasn't there. (be needs no did)

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
Match the present: wherever you'd say is, use was; wherever you'd say are, use were.
⭐
Never use did with be. The negative is wasn't / weren't, not "didn't was".
🔊
was is usually weak: /wəz/. It only sounds strong (/wɒz/) in short answers — "Yes, I was."

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
was / were = the past of be.

I/he/she/it→was·you/we/they→were
  • was for I/he/she/it; were for you/we/they.
  • Negatives: wasn't / weren't — no did.
  • Questions: put was/were first.

ru.title: Знакомство с was / were
ru.html:
was и were — это прошедшее время глагола be. Они говорят нам, как всё было раньше — где вы были, что чувствовали, каким что-то было.

Если am / is / are — это настоящее время, то was / were — просто его прошедший близнец.

🎯
Используйте их со словами прошедшего времени: yesterday, last night, in 2010, two hours ago.

kk.title: was / were етістіктерімен танысу
kk.html:
was және were — be етістігінің өткен шақтағы түрі. Олар заттардың бұрын қандай болғанын айтады — қайда болғаныңды, өзіңді қалай сезінгеніңді, бір нәрсенің қандай болғанын.

am / is / are — осы шақ болса, was / were — оның өткен шақтағы егізі, сол ғана.

🎯
Оларды өткен шақ мезгіл сөздерімен қолдан: yesterday, last night, in 2010, two hours ago.

ru.title: Когда это используем?
ru.html:
📍 Место в прошлом
I was at home yesterday.
😊 Чувство в прошлом
They were tired after the trip.
🌤️ Описание в прошлом
The weather was lovely.
👶 Возраст / время
She was born in May.
yesterday
●
now

Это состояние завершено — оно принадлежит прошлому.

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
📍 Өткендегі орын
I was at home yesterday.
😊 Өткендегі сезім
They were tired after the trip.
🌤️ Өткендегі сипаттама
The weather was lovely.
👶 Жас / уақыт
She was born in May.
yesterday
●
now

Бұл күй аяқталған — ол өткенге тиесілі.

ru.title: Структура — всего два слова
ru.html:
I / he / she / it+was
you / we / they+were

Отрицание, вопрос и краткие ответы

+He was late.
−He wasn't late.
?Was he late?
краткий ответYes, he was. / No, he wasn't.

Для you/we/they: weren't и Were …?

kk.title: Құрылымы — бар болғаны екі сөз
kk.html:
I / he / she / it+was
you / we / they+were

Болымсыз түр, сұрақ және қысқа жауаптар

+He was late.
−He wasn't late.
?Was he late?
қысқа жауапYes, he was. / No, he wasn't.

you/we/they үшін: weren't және Were …?

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Where were you last night?
I was at the cinema. The film was great!
Were your friends there too?
Yes, they were. We were all together.
🏖️ Отпуск
"The beach was beautiful and the people were friendly."
🎂 Детство
"When I was six, we were very happy."

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Where were you last night?
I was at the cinema. The film was great!
Were your friends there too?
Yes, they were. We were all together.
🏖️ Демалыс
"The beach was beautiful and the people were friendly."
🎂 Балалық шақ
"When I was six, we were very happy."

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
You was late.  ✓ You were late.
✗
They was happy.  ✓ They were happy.
✗
Was you at home?  ✓ Were you at home?
✗
I didn't was there.  ✓ I wasn't there. (be не требует did)

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
You was late.  ✓ You were late.
✗
They was happy.  ✓ They were happy.
✗
Was you at home?  ✓ Were you at home?
✗
I didn't was there.  ✓ I wasn't there. (be етістігіне did қажет емес)

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Сопоставьте с настоящим: там, где вы сказали бы is, используйте was; там, где сказали бы are, используйте were.
⭐
Никогда не используйте did с be. Отрицание — это wasn't / weren't, а не «didn't was».
🔊
was обычно произносится слабо: /wəz/. Сильно (/wɒz/) оно звучит только в кратких ответах — «Yes, I was.»

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Осы шаққа сәйкестендір: is дейтін жерде was қолдан; are дейтін жерде were қолдан.
⭐
be етістігімен ешқашан did қолданба. Болымсыз түрі — wasn't / weren't, «didn't was» емес.
🔊
was әдетте әлсіз айтылады: /wəz/. Ол тек қысқа жауаптарда күшті естіледі (/wɒz/) — «Yes, I was.»

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
was / were = прошедшее время глагола be.

I/he/she/it→was·you/we/they→were
  • was для I/he/she/it; were для you/we/they.
  • Отрицания: wasn't / weren't — без did.
  • Вопросы: ставьте was/were на первое место.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
was / were = be етістігінің өткен шағы.

I/he/she/it→was·you/we/they→were
  • I/he/she/it үшін was; you/we/they үшін were.
  • Болымсыз түрі: wasn't / weren't — did жоқ.
  • Сұрақтар: was/were сөзін алдына қой.

activities.prompt: Which past form of be goes with each subject?

why: Correct — the negative of was is wasn't.

activities.instruction: Write where you were yesterday evening.

activities.scene: A friend asks about your weekend.

options.t: I was at the cinema. The film was great!
I were at the cinema.
Yes, they were.
Yes, they was.

activities.why: You used was/were correctly in the past.

learn.title: Meet the past simple
learn.html:
The past simple is the tense for finished actions. Something happened, it is over, and we know when.

Most verbs just add -ed (worked, played, started). A group of common verbs are irregular and change completely (go → went).

🎯
Signal words: yesterday, last week, in 2019, an hour ago, when I was young.

learn.title: When do we use it?
learn.html:
✅ Finished action
I watched a film last night.
📖 A sequence of events
She woke up, dressed and left.
🕰️ Past habits
We played outside every day.
📅 A specific past time
They married in 2015.
●
finished
●
now

learn.title: Structure — add -ed (or learn the irregular)
learn.html:
subject+verb + ed

Spelling of regular -ed

most verbswork → worked
ends in -elive → lived
consonant + ystudy → studied
short vowel + consonantstop → stopped

Common irregular verbs (learn these by heart):

go → wenthave → hadsee → saw
get → gotmake → madecome → came
take → tookeat → atebuy → bought

Good news: the form is the same for every subject — I/you/he/we/they all say went.

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
How was your weekend?
Great! I visited my parents, we cooked together and I slept a lot.
Nice — did you relax?
Yes! I read a whole book.
🧳 Story
"We arrived, found the hotel and had dinner."
💼 Report
"The company grew and opened two offices."

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I goed home.  ✓ I went home. (go is irregular)
✗
She studyed.  ✓ She studied. (y → ied)
✗
We was there.  ✓ We were there.
✗
He buyed a car.  ✓ He bought a car.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🔊
Saying -ed: /t/ after voiceless sounds (worked), /d/ after voiced (played), and /ɪd/ only after t or d (wanted, needed).
🧠
One form for all: the past simple never changes for he/she/it — no extra -s.
⭐
Keep a personal list of irregular verbs and review it often — there are about 100 common ones.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
Past simple = a finished action at a known past time.

subject+-ed / irregular
  • Regular verbs add -ed; irregular verbs change (go→went).
  • Same form for every subject.
  • Watch the spelling: study→studied, stop→stopped.

ru.title: Знакомство с Past Simple
ru.html:
Past Simple (прошедшее простое) — это время для завершённых действий. Что-то произошло, оно закончилось, и мы знаем, когда.

Большинство глаголов просто добавляют -ed (worked, played, started). Группа распространённых глаголов является неправильной (irregular) и меняется полностью (go → went).

🎯
Слова-сигналы: yesterday, last week, in 2019, an hour ago, when I was young.

kk.title: Past Simple шағымен танысу
kk.html:
Past Simple (жедел өткен шақ) — аяқталған әрекеттердің шағы. Бір нәрсе болып өтті, ол бітті және біз оның қашан болғанын білеміз.

Көптеген етістіктер тек -ed жалғауын қосады (worked, played, started). Жиі қолданылатын бір топ етістік дұрыс емес (irregular) және мүлдем өзгереді (go → went).

🎯
Белгі сөздер: yesterday, last week, in 2019, an hour ago, when I was young.

ru.title: Когда это используем?
ru.html:
✅ Завершённое действие
I watched a film last night.
📖 Последовательность событий
She woke up, dressed and left.
🕰️ Привычки в прошлом
We played outside every day.
📅 Конкретное время в прошлом
They married in 2015.
●
finished
●
now

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
✅ Аяқталған әрекет
I watched a film last night.
📖 Оқиғалар тізбегі
She woke up, dressed and left.
🕰️ Өткендегі әдеттер
We played outside every day.
📅 Нақты өткен уақыт
They married in 2015.
●
finished
●
now

ru.title: Структура — добавь -ed (или выучи неправильный глагол)
ru.html:
subject+verb + ed

Написание правильного -ed

большинство глаголовwork → worked
оканчивается на -elive → lived
согласная + ystudy → studied
краткая гласная + согласнаяstop → stopped

Распространённые неправильные глаголы (выучите их наизусть):

go → wenthave → hadsee → saw
get → gotmake → madecome → came
take → tookeat → atebuy → bought

Хорошая новость: форма одинакова для каждого подлежащего — I/you/he/we/they все говорят went.

kk.title: Құрылымы — -ed қос (немесе дұрыс емес түрін үйрен)
kk.html:
subject+verb + ed

Дұрыс -ed жалғауының жазылуы

көптеген етістіктерwork → worked
-e-ге аяқталадыlive → lived
дауыссыз + ystudy → studied
қысқа дауысты + дауыссызstop → stopped

Жиі кездесетін дұрыс емес етістіктер (жатқа үйрен):

go → wenthave → hadsee → saw
get → gotmake → madecome → came
take → tookeat → atebuy → bought

Жақсы жаңалық: форма әр бастауыш үшін бірдей — I/you/he/we/they бәрі went дейді.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
How was your weekend?
Great! I visited my parents, we cooked together and I slept a lot.
Nice — did you relax?
Yes! I read a whole book.
🧳 История
"We arrived, found the hotel and had dinner."
💼 Отчёт
"The company grew and opened two offices."

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
How was your weekend?
Great! I visited my parents, we cooked together and I slept a lot.
Nice — did you relax?
Yes! I read a whole book.
🧳 Әңгіме
"We arrived, found the hotel and had dinner."
💼 Есеп
"The company grew and opened two offices."

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
I goed home.  ✓ I went home. (go — неправильный глагол)
✗
She studyed.  ✓ She studied. (y → ied)
✗
We was there.  ✓ We were there.
✗
He buyed a car.  ✓ He bought a car.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
I goed home.  ✓ I went home. (go — дұрыс емес етістік)
✗
She studyed.  ✓ She studied. (y → ied)
✗
We was there.  ✓ We were there.
✗
He buyed a car.  ✓ He bought a car.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🔊
Произношение -ed: /t/ после глухих звуков (worked), /d/ после звонких (played) и /ɪd/ только после t или d (wanted, needed).
🧠
Одна форма для всех: Past Simple никогда не меняется для he/she/it — никакого лишнего -s.
⭐
Ведите личный список неправильных глаголов и почаще повторяйте его — распространённых около 100.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🔊
-ed жалғауын айту: ұяң емес дыбыстардан кейін /t/ (worked), ұяң дыбыстардан кейін /d/ (played), ал /ɪd/ тек t немесе d-дан кейін (wanted, needed).
🧠
Бәріне бір форма: Past Simple he/she/it үшін ешқашан өзгермейді — қосымша -s жоқ.
⭐
Дұрыс емес етістіктердің жеке тізімін жаса да, жиі қайтала — олардың шамамен 100 жиі кездесетіні бар.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
Past Simple = завершённое действие в известное прошедшее время.

subject+-ed / irregular
  • Правильные глаголы добавляют -ed; неправильные меняются (go→went).
  • Одинаковая форма для каждого подлежащего.
  • Следите за написанием: study→studied, stop→stopped.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
Past Simple = белгілі өткен уақыттағы аяқталған әрекет.

subject+-ed / irregular
  • Дұрыс етістіктер -ed қосады; дұрыс емес етістіктер өзгереді (go→went).
  • Әр бастауыш үшін бірдей форма.
  • Жазылуын байқа: study→studied, stop→stopped.

activities.prompt: Match each base verb to its past simple.
activities.why: These are common irregular past forms — learn them by heart.
Regular verbs add -ed; irregular verbs change form (go→went).
Subject + past verb + rest: “We watched a film last night.”
/t/ after voiceless sounds, /d/ after voiced, /ɪd/ only after t or d.

activities.instruction: Write one thing you did yesterday (past simple).
activities.why: Use a past-simple verb — e.g. “I went to work / watched a film.”

activities.scene: A friend asks about your weekend.

steps.npc: What did you do at the weekend?

options.t: I visited my parents and cooked with them.
I visit my parents and cook with them.
options.why: “at the weekend” (past) → past simple: visited, cooked.

activities.why: You told a short past story.

learn.title: Past simple — negatives & questions
learn.html:
To make the past simple negative or a question, we use one helper: did (the past of do).

Here's the magic: once did or didn't appears, the main verb goes back to its base form — no more -ed, no irregular past.

🎯
did already carries the past, so the main verb can relax: Did you go? — not "Did you went?"

learn.title: When do we use it?
learn.html:
🚫 Say what didn't happen
I didn't see her.
❓ Ask about the past
Did you call him?
🔎 Wh- questions
What did you eat?

learn.title: Structure — did does the work
learn.html:
Negative:

subject+didn't+base verb

Question:

Did+subject+base verb ?

See it change back to base

+She went out.
−She didn't go out.
?Did she go out?
short answerYes, she did. / No, she didn't.

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Did you finish the report?
No, I didn't have time. I didn't even start it!
What did you do instead?
I helped a customer for two hours.
🕵️ Checking facts
"Did he call you back?"
🙅 Correcting
"No, I didn't say that."

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I didn't went.  ✓ I didn't go. (base after didn't)
✗
Did you saw it?  ✓ Did you see it?
✗
She no came.  ✓ She didn't come.
✗
Where you went?  ✓ Where did you go?

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
One past is enough: if did/didn't is there, the main verb is never in the past form.
⭐
Exception — be: was/were make their own negatives and questions (wasn't, Were you…?). They never use did.
🗣️
In speech, didn't is very common; the full "did not" sounds formal or emphatic.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
Negatives & questions in the past use did / didn't + base verb.

Did / didn't+base verb
  • After did/didn't the verb loses its past form.
  • Short answers: Yes, I did. / No, I didn't.
  • be is the exception — use wasn't/weren't, Was/Were…?

ru.title: Past Simple — отрицания и вопросы
ru.html:
Чтобы сделать Past Simple отрицательным или вопросом, мы используем один помощник: did (прошедшее время do).

Вот в чём секрет: как только появляется did или didn't, главный глагол возвращается к начальной форме — больше никакого -ed, никакого неправильного прошедшего.

🎯
did уже несёт прошедшее время, поэтому главный глагол может расслабиться: Did you go? — а не «Did you went?»

kk.title: Past Simple — болымсыз түр және сұрақтар
kk.html:
Past Simple-ды болымсыз түрге немесе сұраққа айналдыру үшін бір көмекші қолданамыз: did (do-ның өткен шағы).

Міне сиқыры: did немесе didn't пайда болса, негізгі етістік өзінің негізгі тұлғасына қайтады — енді -ed те, дұрыс емес өткен шақ та жоқ.

🎯
did өткен шақты өзі көтеріп тұрады, сондықтан негізгі етістік тынығуына болады: Did you go? — «Did you went?» емес.

ru.title: Когда это используем?
ru.html:
🚫 Сказать, что не произошло
I didn't see her.
❓ Спросить о прошлом
Did you call him?
🔎 Wh- вопросы
What did you eat?

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
🚫 Не болмағанын айту
I didn't see her.
❓ Өткен туралы сұрау
Did you call him?
🔎 Wh- сұрақтары
What did you eat?

ru.title: Структура — did делает всю работу
ru.html:
Отрицание:

subject+didn't+base verb

Вопрос:

Did+subject+base verb ?

Посмотри, как он возвращается к начальной форме

+She went out.
−She didn't go out.
?Did she go out?
краткий ответYes, she did. / No, she didn't.

kk.title: Құрылымы — did бәрін атқарады
kk.html:
Болымсыз түр:

subject+didn't+base verb

Сұрақ:

Did+subject+base verb ?

Негізгі тұлғаға қайтқанын көр

+She went out.
−She didn't go out.
?Did she go out?
қысқа жауапYes, she did. / No, she didn't.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Did you finish the report?
No, I didn't have time. I didn't even start it!
What did you do instead?
I helped a customer for two hours.
🕵️ Проверка фактов
"Did he call you back?"
🙅 Исправление
"No, I didn't say that."

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Did you finish the report?
No, I didn't have time. I didn't even start it!
What did you do instead?
I helped a customer for two hours.
🕵️ Фактілерді тексеру
"Did he call you back?"
🙅 Түзету
"No, I didn't say that."

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
I didn't went.  ✓ I didn't go. (начальная форма после didn't)
✗
Did you saw it?  ✓ Did you see it?
✗
She no came.  ✓ She didn't come.
✗
Where you went?  ✓ Where did you go?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
I didn't went.  ✓ I didn't go. (didn't-тен кейін негізгі тұлға)
✗
Did you saw it?  ✓ Did you see it?
✗
She no came.  ✓ She didn't come.
✗
Where you went?  ✓ Where did you go?

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Одного прошедшего достаточно: если есть did/didn't, главный глагол никогда не стоит в прошедшей форме.
⭐
Исключение — be: was/were образуют свои отрицания и вопросы сами (wasn't, Were you…?). Они никогда не используют did.
🗣️
В речи didn't встречается очень часто; полное «did not» звучит официально или подчёркнуто.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Бір өткен шақ жеткілікті: did/didn't тұрса, негізгі етістік ешқашан өткен шақ тұлғасында болмайды.
⭐
Ерекшелік — be: was/were өз болымсыз түрі мен сұрақтарын өздері жасайды (wasn't, Were you…?). Олар ешқашан did қолданбайды.
🗣️
Сөйлеу тілінде didn't өте жиі кездеседі; толық «did not» ресми немесе екпінді естіледі.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
Отрицания и вопросы в прошедшем времени используют did / didn't + начальную форму глагола.

Did / didn't+base verb
  • После did/didn't глагол теряет свою прошедшую форму.
  • Краткие ответы: Yes, I did. / No, I didn't.
  • be — исключение: используйте wasn't/weren't, Was/Were…?

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
Өткен шақтағы болымсыз түр мен сұрақтар did / didn't + негізгі етістікті қолданады.

Did / didn't+base verb
  • did/didn't-тен кейін етістік өткен шақ тұлғасын жоғалтады.
  • Қысқа жауаптар: Yes, I did. / No, I didn't.
  • be — ерекшелік: wasn't/weren't, Was/Were…? қолдан.

activities.why: After did/didn't the verb goes back to its base form.
Did + subject + base verb: “Did you finish the report?”
base verb after didn't: “I didn't go out.”
base verb after did: “Did you see it?”

activities.q: “Did they win?” — Which reply is correct?

activities.instruction: Ask a friend what they ate for lunch.
activities.answer: what did you eat for lunch
alts: what did you have for lunch
what did you eat for lunch?

activities.scene: You check in with a colleague.

options.t: Did you finish the project?
Did you finished the project?

steps.npc: Not yet. (Ask why they didn't call.)

options.t: Why didn't you call me?
Why you didn't called me?

activities.why: You asked and answered past questions correctly.

learn.title: Meet the past continuous
learn.html:
The past continuous shows an action that was in progress at a moment in the past — it had started but not finished.

Form: was / were + verb-ing. Think of a photo of the past: something was happening in it.

🎯
"At 8 p.m. I was having dinner." — the meal was in the middle of happening.

learn.title: When do we use it?
learn.html:
⏱️ In progress at a past time
At nine I was working.
🎬 Background scene
The sun was shining and birds were singing.
✂️ Interrupted action
I was sleeping when the phone rang.
🔀 Two actions together
She was reading while he was cooking.
start
in progress
end

learn.title: Structure — was/were + -ing
learn.html:
I / he / she / it+was+verb-ing
you / we / they+were+verb-ing

Negative, question & short answers

+They were playing.
−They weren't playing.
?Were they playing?
short answerYes, they were. / No, they weren't.

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
I called you at eight — you didn't answer.
Sorry! I was driving. I wasn't looking at my phone.
No problem. What were you doing later?
We were having dinner.
📖 Story opening
"It was raining and the streets were empty."
🚨 Interruption
"I was cooking when the alarm went off."

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I was work.  ✓ I was working. (need -ing)
✗
They was playing.  ✓ They were playing.
✗
I was knowing the answer.  ✓ I knew the answer. (state verbs aren't continuous)

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
Formula memory: it's the present continuous with was/were swapped in for am/is/are.
⭐
State verbs (know, like, want, believe) don't take -ing — use the past simple instead.
🗣️
It's the classic way to set the scene at the start of a story: "The wind was blowing…"

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
Past continuous = an action in progress at a past moment.

was / were+verb-ing
  • Use for actions in progress, background scenes, and interruptions.
  • was for I/he/she/it; were for you/we/they.
  • State verbs stay in the past simple.

ru.title: Знакомство с Past Continuous
ru.html:
Past Continuous показывает действие, которое было в процессе в определённый момент в прошлом — оно началось, но не закончилось.

Форма: was / were + verb-ing. Представьте фотографию прошлого: на ней что-то происходило.

🎯
«At 8 p.m. I was having dinner.» — обед был в самом разгаре.

kk.title: Past Continuous шағымен танысу
kk.html:
Past Continuous — өткендегі бір сәтте болып жатқан әрекетті көрсетеді — ол басталған, бірақ аяқталмаған.

Тұлғасы: was / were + verb-ing. Өткеннің суретін елестет: онда бір нәрсе болып жатыр еді.

🎯
«At 8 p.m. I was having dinner.» — тамақ дәл ортасында болып жатқан еді.

ru.title: Когда это используем?
ru.html:
⏱️ В процессе в момент в прошлом
At nine I was working.
🎬 Фоновая сцена
The sun was shining and birds were singing.
✂️ Прерванное действие
I was sleeping when the phone rang.
🔀 Два действия одновременно
She was reading while he was cooking.
start
in progress
end

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
⏱️ Өткен уақытта болып жатқан
At nine I was working.
🎬 Фондық көрініс
The sun was shining and birds were singing.
✂️ Үзілген әрекет
I was sleeping when the phone rang.
🔀 Қатар жүрген екі әрекет
She was reading while he was cooking.
start
in progress
end

ru.title: Структура — was/were + -ing
ru.html:
I / he / she / it+was+verb-ing
you / we / they+were+verb-ing

Отрицание, вопрос и краткие ответы

+They were playing.
−They weren't playing.
?Were they playing?
краткий ответYes, they were. / No, they weren't.

kk.title: Құрылымы — was/were + -ing
kk.html:
I / he / she / it+was+verb-ing
you / we / they+were+verb-ing

Болымсыз түр, сұрақ және қысқа жауаптар

+They were playing.
−They weren't playing.
?Were they playing?
қысқа жауапYes, they were. / No, they weren't.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
I called you at eight — you didn't answer.
Sorry! I was driving. I wasn't looking at my phone.
No problem. What were you doing later?
We were having dinner.
📖 Начало истории
"It was raining and the streets were empty."
🚨 Прерывание
"I was cooking when the alarm went off."

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
I called you at eight — you didn't answer.
Sorry! I was driving. I wasn't looking at my phone.
No problem. What were you doing later?
We were having dinner.
📖 Әңгіменің басы
"It was raining and the streets were empty."
🚨 Үзіліс
"I was cooking when the alarm went off."

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
I was work.  ✓ I was working. (нужно -ing)
✗
They was playing.  ✓ They were playing.
✗
I was knowing the answer.  ✓ I knew the answer. (глаголы состояния не бывают в продолженном)

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
I was work.  ✓ I was working. (-ing қажет)
✗
They was playing.  ✓ They were playing.
✗
I was knowing the answer.  ✓ I knew the answer. (күй етістіктері созылыңқы түрде болмайды)

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Как запомнить формулу: это Present Continuous, где am/is/are заменены на was/were.
⭐
Глаголы состояния (know, like, want, believe) не принимают -ing — вместо этого используйте Past Simple.
🗣️
Это классический способ задать сцену в начале истории: «The wind was blowing…»

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Тұлғаны есте сақтау: бұл — Present Continuous, тек am/is/are орнына was/were қойылған.
⭐
Күй етістіктері (know, like, want, believe) -ing жалғауын қабылдамайды — оның орнына Past Simple қолдан.
🗣️
Бұл — әңгіме басында көрініс орнатудың классикалық тәсілі: «The wind was blowing…»

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
Past Continuous = действие в процессе в момент в прошлом.

was / were+verb-ing
  • Используйте для действий в процессе, фоновых сцен и прерываний.
  • was для I/he/she/it; were для you/we/they.
  • Глаголы состояния остаются в Past Simple.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
Past Continuous = өткендегі бір сәтте болып жатқан әрекет.

was / were+verb-ing
  • Болып жатқан әрекеттер, фондық көріністер мен үзілістер үшін қолдан.
  • I/he/she/it үшін was; you/we/they үшін were.
  • Күй етістіктері Past Simple-де қалады.

activities.why: Past continuous = an action in progress at a moment in the past.
was/were + verb-ing; match was/were to the subject.
was/were + verb-ing: “She was reading a book.”

activities.q: “I called at eight, but you didn't answer.” — “Sorry, I ___.”

why: know is a state verb — not continuous.

activities.instruction: Write what you were doing at 8 p.m. yesterday.

activities.scene: You describe a moment from last night.

steps.npc: What were you doing when I texted you?

options.t: I was cooking dinner.
I cooked dinner.
options.why: For an action in progress use “I was cooking”.

activities.why: You described a past action in progress.

learn.title: Past continuous vs past simple
learn.html:
These two past tenses work as a team. The past continuous sets the longer background; the past simple is the shorter, completed action that happens inside it.

"I was walking home when I met Tom." — the walk was long and in progress; meeting Tom was quick and finished.

learn.title: When do we use each?
learn.html:
Past continuous (background)Past simple (completed)
a longer action in progressa short, finished action
the scene / the situationthe event that happened
often after whileoften after when
was walking
met Tom
kept walking

The simple action interrupts the continuous one.

learn.title: Compare the pair
learn.html:
I was cooking when the phone rang.cooking = background · rang = event
While she was studying, the lights went out.studying = long · went out = sudden
When the film started, we sat down.two short actions → both simple
🔍
when + short action (past simple); while + long action (past continuous).

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
How did you break your arm?
I was playing football when I fell.
Ouch! What were you doing before that?
We were just warming up when it happened.
📖 Storytelling
"The sun was setting when we arrived."
🗞️ News
"He was crossing the road when a car stopped for him."

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
When she called, I cooked.  ✓ When she called, I was cooking. (the background was in progress)
✗
While I was walking, I was meeting Tom.  ✓ While I was walking, I met Tom.
✗
I was seeing an accident.  ✓ I saw an accident. (a quick, complete event)

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
The rule of two: long action → continuous; the thing that cuts in → simple.
⭐
If both actions are short and one follows the other, use the past simple for both: "I opened the door and went in."
🗣️
Two long actions at the same time both take the continuous: "I was cooking while he was cleaning."

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
Continuous = the background; simple = the event inside it.

was doing+ when +did
  • Long/background action → past continuous.
  • Short/completed action → past simple.
  • when + simple; while + continuous.

ru.title: Past Continuous против Past Simple
ru.html:
Эти два прошедших времени работают как команда. Past Continuous задаёт более длинный фон; Past Simple — это более короткое, завершённое действие, которое происходит внутри него.

«I was walking home when I met Tom.» — прогулка была долгой и в процессе; встреча с Томом была быстрой и завершённой.

kk.title: Past Continuous пен Past Simple
kk.html:
Бұл екі өткен шақ жұп болып жұмыс істейді. Past Continuous ұзағырақ фонды орнатады; Past Simple — оның ішінде болатын қысқарақ, аяқталған әрекет.

«I was walking home when I met Tom.» — жүру ұзақ әрі болып жатқан еді; Томды кездестіру жылдам әрі аяқталған.

ru.title: Когда используем каждую форму?
ru.html:
Past Continuous (фон)Past Simple (завершённое)
более длинное действие в процессекороткое, завершённое действие
сцена / ситуацияпроизошедшее событие
часто после whileчасто после when
was walking
met Tom
kept walking

Простое действие прерывает продолженное.

kk.title: Әрқайсысын қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
Past Continuous (фон)Past Simple (аяқталған)
болып жатқан ұзағырақ әрекетқысқа, аяқталған әрекет
көрініс / жағдайболып өткен оқиға
көбіне while-дан кейінкөбіне when-нен кейін
was walking
met Tom
kept walking

Жедел әрекет созылыңқы әрекетті үзеді.

ru.title: Сравни пару
ru.html:
I was cooking when the phone rang.cooking = фон · rang = событие
While she was studying, the lights went out.studying = долгое · went out = внезапное
When the film started, we sat down.два коротких действия → оба simple
🔍
when + короткое действие (Past Simple); while + долгое действие (Past Continuous).

kk.title: Екеуін салыстыр
kk.html:
I was cooking when the phone rang.cooking = фон · rang = оқиға
While she was studying, the lights went out.studying = ұзақ · went out = кенет
When the film started, we sat down.қысқа екі әрекет → екеуі де simple
🔍
when + қысқа әрекет (Past Simple); while + ұзақ әрекет (Past Continuous).

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
How did you break your arm?
I was playing football when I fell.
Ouch! What were you doing before that?
We were just warming up when it happened.
📖 Рассказ
"The sun was setting when we arrived."
🗞️ Новости
"He was crossing the road when a car stopped for him."

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
How did you break your arm?
I was playing football when I fell.
Ouch! What were you doing before that?
We were just warming up when it happened.
📖 Әңгімелеу
"The sun was setting when we arrived."
🗞️ Жаңалық
"He was crossing the road when a car stopped for him."

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
When she called, I cooked.  ✓ When she called, I was cooking. (фон был в процессе)
✗
While I was walking, I was meeting Tom.  ✓ While I was walking, I met Tom.
✗
I was seeing an accident.  ✓ I saw an accident. (быстрое, завершённое событие)

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
When she called, I cooked.  ✓ When she called, I was cooking. (фон болып жатқан еді)
✗
While I was walking, I was meeting Tom.  ✓ While I was walking, I met Tom.
✗
I was seeing an accident.  ✓ I saw an accident. (жылдам, аяқталған оқиға)

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Правило двух: долгое действие → continuous; то, что вклинивается → simple.
⭐
Если оба действия короткие и одно следует за другим, используйте Past Simple для обоих: «I opened the door and went in.»
🗣️
Два долгих действия одновременно оба принимают continuous: «I was cooking while he was cleaning.»

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Екеудің ережесі: ұзақ әрекет → continuous; қиып өтетін нәрсе → simple.
⭐
Екі әрекет те қысқа әрі бірінен соң бірі болса, екеуіне де Past Simple қолдан: «I opened the door and went in.»
🗣️
Бір мезгілде болатын екі ұзақ әрекеттің екеуі де continuous-ты алады: «I was cooking while he was cleaning.»

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
Continuous = фон; simple = событие внутри него.

was doing+ when +did
  • Долгое/фоновое действие → Past Continuous.
  • Короткое/завершённое действие → Past Simple.
  • when + simple; while + continuous.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
Continuous = фон; simple = оның ішіндегі оқиға.

was doing+ when +did
  • Ұзақ/фондық әрекет → Past Continuous.
  • Қысқа/аяқталған әрекет → Past Simple.
  • when + simple; while + continuous.

items.s: I was walking home when I met Tom.
I walked home when I was meeting Tom.
While she was studying, the lights went out.
While she studied, the lights were going out.

activities.why: Long background → continuous; short completed action → past simple.

activities.before: I was cooking when the phone
activities.why: the short interrupting action → past simple: rang.
the long background action → past continuous: was studying.
the background was in progress: “…I was cooking.”

why: Correct — “I was playing football when I fell.”

activities.q: “___ I was walking home, it started to rain.”
why: Correct — a long action → While I was walking…
During takes a noun, not a clause.

activities.why: Continuous = the ongoing scene; simple = the event that happens.

activities.prompt: When the film started, we ___ (sit) down.
activities.why: Two short actions → both past simple: “…we sat down.”

activities.scene: A friend asks how you hurt your arm.

steps.npc: How did you break your arm?

options.t: I was playing football when I fell.
I played football when I was falling.
options.why: Long action → was playing; short event → fell.

activities.why: You combined the two past tenses correctly.

learn.title: Meet the present perfect
learn.html:
The present perfect links the past to now. It talks about a past action that matters at this moment — often we don't say exactly when it happened, because the result is what counts.

Form: have / has + the past participle (done, been, seen, gone…).

🎯
"I have lost my keys." → past action, present result: I can't get in now.

learn.title: When do we use it?
learn.html:
🎁 Past action, present result
She has broken her leg. (it's still broken)
🌍 Life experience (unstated time)
I have been to Japan.
🆕 Recent news
They have just arrived.
⏳ Unfinished time (today, this year)
I have seen three films this week.
past ?
→ result
NOW

learn.title: Structure — have/has + past participle
learn.html:
I / you / we / they+have+past participle
he / she / it+has+past participle

Negative, question & short answers

+I have seen it. / She has gone.
−I haven't seen it. / She hasn't gone.
?Have you seen it? / Has she gone?
short answerYes, I have. / No, she hasn't.

Regular participles = -ed (worked). Irregular ones must be learned: see→seen, go→gone, do→done.

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Do you want to see this film?
I've already seen it, but it's great!
Have you eaten?
Yes, I've just had lunch.
🧳 Experience
"I've never flown before."
🔧 Result now
"He's fixed the car — it works!"

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I have see it.  ✓ I have seen it. (participle, not base)
✗
She have gone.  ✓ She has gone.
✗
I have seen it yesterday.  ✓ I saw it yesterday. (a finished time → past simple)

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
The bridge tense: present perfect connects then and now. If a finished time word (yesterday, in 2010) is there, switch to the past simple.
🗣️
In speech we contract: I've, you've, he's, she's. "She's gone" = she has gone.
⭐
Keep an irregular-participle list: be→been, have→had, take→taken, give→given.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
Present perfect = a past action connected to now.

have / has+past participle
  • Use it for results, experiences and recent news — time not stated.
  • has for he/she/it; have for the rest.
  • With a finished time (yesterday), use the past simple instead.

ru.title: Знакомство с Present Perfect
ru.html:
Present Perfect связывает прошлое с настоящим. Оно говорит о прошлом действии, которое важно в данный момент — часто мы не говорим точно, когда оно произошло, потому что важен результат.

Форма: have / has + причастие прошедшего времени (V3) (done, been, seen, gone…).

🎯
«I have lost my keys.» → прошлое действие, настоящий результат: я сейчас не могу войти.

kk.title: Present Perfect шағымен танысу
kk.html:
Present Perfect өткенді қазіргімен байланыстырады. Ол дәл осы сәтте маңызы бар өткен әрекет туралы айтады — көбіне оның қашан болғанын дәл айтпаймыз, өйткені нәтижесі маңызды.

Тұлғасы: have / has + өткен шақ есімшесі (done, been, seen, gone…).

🎯
«I have lost my keys.» → өткен әрекет, қазіргі нәтиже: мен қазір ішке кіре алмаймын.

ru.title: Когда это используем?
ru.html:
🎁 Прошлое действие, настоящий результат
She has broken her leg. (она всё ещё сломана)
🌍 Жизненный опыт (время не указано)
I have been to Japan.
🆕 Свежие новости
They have just arrived.
⏳ Незавершённое время (today, this year)
I have seen three films this week.
past ?
→ result
NOW

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
🎁 Өткен әрекет, қазіргі нәтиже
She has broken her leg. (әлі сынық)
🌍 Өмірлік тәжірибе (уақыты айтылмаған)
I have been to Japan.
🆕 Соңғы жаңалық
They have just arrived.
⏳ Аяқталмаған уақыт (today, this year)
I have seen three films this week.
past ?
→ result
NOW

ru.title: Структура — have/has + причастие прошедшего времени (V3)
ru.html:
I / you / we / they+have+past participle
he / she / it+has+past participle

Отрицание, вопрос и краткие ответы

+I have seen it. / She has gone.
−I haven't seen it. / She hasn't gone.
?Have you seen it? / Has she gone?
краткий ответYes, I have. / No, she hasn't.

Правильные причастия = -ed (worked). Неправильные нужно выучить: see→seen, go→gone, do→done.

kk.title: Құрылымы — have/has + өткен шақ есімшесі
kk.html:
I / you / we / they+have+past participle
he / she / it+has+past participle

Болымсыз түр, сұрақ және қысқа жауаптар

+I have seen it. / She has gone.
−I haven't seen it. / She hasn't gone.
?Have you seen it? / Has she gone?
қысқа жауапYes, I have. / No, she hasn't.

Дұрыс есімшелер = -ed (worked). Дұрыс еместерін жаттау керек: see→seen, go→gone, do→done.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Do you want to see this film?
I've already seen it, but it's great!
Have you eaten?
Yes, I've just had lunch.
🧳 Опыт
"I've never flown before."
🔧 Результат сейчас
"He's fixed the car — it works!"

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Do you want to see this film?
I've already seen it, but it's great!
Have you eaten?
Yes, I've just had lunch.
🧳 Тәжірибе
"I've never flown before."
🔧 Қазіргі нәтиже
"He's fixed the car — it works!"

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
I have see it.  ✓ I have seen it. (причастие, а не начальная форма)
✗
She have gone.  ✓ She has gone.
✗
I have seen it yesterday.  ✓ I saw it yesterday. (завершённое время → Past Simple)

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
I have see it.  ✓ I have seen it. (есімше, негізгі тұлға емес)
✗
She have gone.  ✓ She has gone.
✗
I have seen it yesterday.  ✓ I saw it yesterday. (аяқталған уақыт → Past Simple)

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Время-мост: Present Perfect соединяет тогда и сейчас. Если есть слово завершённого времени (yesterday, in 2010), переходите на Past Simple.
🗣️
В речи мы сокращаем: I've, you've, he's, she's. «She's gone» = she has gone.
⭐
Ведите список неправильных причастий: be→been, have→had, take→taken, give→given.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Көпір шақ: Present Perfect өткенді мен қазіргіні байланыстырады. Аяқталған уақыт сөзі (yesterday, in 2010) болса, Past Simple-ге ауыс.
🗣️
Сөйлеу тілінде қысқартамыз: I've, you've, he's, she's. «She's gone» = she has gone.
⭐
Дұрыс емес есімшелердің тізімін жүргіз: be→been, have→had, take→taken, give→given.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
Present Perfect = прошлое действие, связанное с настоящим.

have / has+past participle
  • Используйте для результатов, опыта и свежих новостей — время не указано.
  • has для he/she/it; have для остальных.
  • С завершённым временем (yesterday) используйте Past Simple.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
Present Perfect = қазіргімен байланысты өткен әрекет.

have / has+past participle
  • Нәтижелер, тәжірибе және соңғы жаңалықтар үшін қолдан — уақыты айтылмайды.
  • he/she/it үшін has; қалғандары үшін have.
  • Аяқталған уақытпен (yesterday) оның орнына Past Simple қолдан.

activities.prompt: Match each verb to its past participle.
activities.why: The present perfect uses have/has + past participle — learn the irregular ones.
I + have + been + rest: “I have been to Japan.”
present perfect needs the participle: “He has gone home.”

why: Past simple states when; here we focus on the result now.

activities.why: a finished time (yesterday) needs the past simple; the present perfect goes with an unfinished time like today.

activities.instruction: Write one place you have been to (experience).
activities.why: I + have been to + place — e.g. “I've been to Italy.”

activities.scene: You have some news for a friend.

options.t: I've passed my driving test!
I pass my driving test!

activities.why: You used the present perfect for recent news.

learn.title: just, already & yet
learn.html:
These three little words make the present perfect precise. They tell us how recent or whether something has happened.

just = a short time ago · already = sooner than expected · yet = up to now (in questions & negatives).

🎯
They all live with have/has + past participle.

learn.title: When do we use each?
learn.html:
🆕 just — very recently
I've just finished.
✅ already — before now / earlier than expected
She's already left.
❓ yet — questions (is it done?)
Have you eaten yet?
🚫 yet — negatives (not up to now)
They haven't arrived yet.

learn.title: Structure — where the word goes
learn.html:
justafter have/has: I've just seen him.
alreadyafter have/has: We've already paid.
yetat the end: Have you finished yet? · Not yet.
⭐
just / already sit in the middle; yet goes at the end.

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Shall we start dinner?
I've just eaten, actually.
Oh — have you paid the bill yet?
Yes, I've already done it.
📦 Delivery
"It hasn't come yet."
🎉 News
"They've just got married!"

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I have already ate.  ✓ I have already eaten.
✗
Have you yet finished?  ✓ Have you finished yet?
✗
I've just went out.  ✓ I've just gone out.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
Position map: just / already = middle · yet = end.
🗣️
"Not yet" is a complete, natural answer: "Have you finished?" — "Not yet."
⭐
yet lives in questions & negatives; already lives in positives.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
just / already / yet fine-tune the present perfect.

  • just = moments ago (middle position).
  • already = sooner than expected (middle, positive).
  • yet = up to now (end, questions & negatives).

ru.title: just, already и yet
ru.html:
Эти три маленьких слова делают Present Perfect точнее. Они говорят нам, насколько недавно или произошло ли что-то.

just = недавно · already = раньше, чем ожидалось · yet = до сих пор (в вопросах и отрицаниях).

🎯
Все они живут с have/has + причастием прошедшего времени.

kk.title: just, already және yet
kk.html:
Осы үш кішкентай сөз Present Perfect-ті дәлдей түседі. Олар бір нәрсенің қаншалықты жақында болғанын немесе болған-болмағанын білдіреді.

just = біраз уақыт бұрын · already = күткеннен ертерек · yet = осы уақытқа дейін (сұрақтар мен болымсыз түрде).

🎯
Олардың бәрі have/has + өткен шақ есімшесімен бірге тұрады.

ru.title: Когда используем каждую форму?
ru.html:
🆕 just — совсем недавно
I've just finished.
✅ already — до сих пор / раньше, чем ожидалось
She's already left.
❓ yet — вопросы (сделано ли?)
Have you eaten yet?
🚫 yet — отрицания (пока нет)
They haven't arrived yet.

kk.title: Әрқайсысын қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
🆕 just — әлдеқашан емес, тап-таяуда
I've just finished.
✅ already — қазірге дейін / күткеннен ертерек
She's already left.
❓ yet — сұрақтар (бітті ме?)
Have you eaten yet?
🚫 yet — болымсыз түр (әзірге жоқ)
They haven't arrived yet.

ru.title: Структура — где стоит слово
ru.html:
justпосле have/has: I've just seen him.
alreadyпосле have/has: We've already paid.
yetв конце: Have you finished yet? · Not yet.
⭐
just / already стоят в середине; yet идёт в конце.

kk.title: Құрылымы — сөз қай жерде тұрады
kk.html:
justhave/has-тен кейін: I've just seen him.
alreadyhave/has-тен кейін: We've already paid.
yetсоңында: Have you finished yet? · Not yet.
⭐
just / already ортада тұрады; yet соңында тұрады.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Shall we start dinner?
I've just eaten, actually.
Oh — have you paid the bill yet?
Yes, I've already done it.
📦 Доставка
"It hasn't come yet."
🎉 Новости
"They've just got married!"

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Shall we start dinner?
I've just eaten, actually.
Oh — have you paid the bill yet?
Yes, I've already done it.
📦 Жеткізу
"It hasn't come yet."
🎉 Жаңалық
"They've just got married!"

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
I have already ate.  ✓ I have already eaten.
✗
Have you yet finished?  ✓ Have you finished yet?
✗
I've just went out.  ✓ I've just gone out.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
I have already ate.  ✓ I have already eaten.
✗
Have you yet finished?  ✓ Have you finished yet?
✗
I've just went out.  ✓ I've just gone out.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Карта позиций: just / already = середина · yet = конец.
🗣️
«Not yet» — полный, естественный ответ: «Have you finished?» — «Not yet.»
⭐
yet живёт в вопросах и отрицаниях; already живёт в утверждениях.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Орналасу картасы: just / already = ортада · yet = соңында.
🗣️
«Not yet» — толық, табиғи жауап: «Have you finished?» — «Not yet.»
⭐
yet сұрақтар мен болымсыз түрде тұрады; already болымды түрде тұрады.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
just / already / yet уточняют Present Perfect.

  • just = мгновения назад (середина).
  • already = раньше, чем ожидалось (середина, утверждение).
  • yet = до сих пор (конец, вопросы и отрицания).

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
just / already / yet Present Perfect-ті нақтылай түседі.

  • just = сәл ғана бұрын (ортадағы орын).
  • already = күткеннен ертерек (ортада, болымды түрде).
  • yet = осы уақытқа дейін (соңында, сұрақтар мен болымсыз түрде).

activities.prompt: Middle (after have/has) or end of the sentence?
activities.why: just and already go in the middle; yet goes at the end.
already + participle (eaten); yet goes at the end.
in a question, use yet at the end: “Have you finished yet?”

activities.q: “They've ___ arrived — they came in a minute ago.”

activities.before: I can't go out — I haven't finished my work
activities.why: negative + up to now → yet at the end.

activities.instruction: Say something you have just done.
activities.why: have + just + participle — e.g. “I've just finished lunch.”

options.t: No, I've already done them, thanks.
No, I've did them yet.

learn.title: Have you ever…? — experiences
learn.html:
We use the present perfect to ask and talk about life experiences — things that have (or haven't) happened at any time in your life. The exact date doesn't matter.

Two key words: ever (= at any time) in questions, and never (= at no time) for the negative.

🎯
"Have you ever been to Paris?" — "No, I've never been."

learn.title: When do we use it?
learn.html:
🌏 Ask about experience
Have you ever eaten sushi?
🚫 No experience
I've never ridden a horse.
🥇 Superlative + ever
It's the best film I've ever seen.

We're looking across your whole life up to now — no specific time.

learn.title: Structure — ever & never
learn.html:
Have / Has+subject+ever+past participle ?
?Have you ever been abroad?
+Yes, I've been to Spain.
−No, I've never been abroad.
⭐
never is already negative — don't add "not": say "I've never been", not "I haven't never been".

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Have you ever tried diving?
No, I've never done it. Have you?
Yes! It's the most amazing thing I've ever done.
💼 Interview
"Have you ever managed a team?"
🍜 Food
"I've never tasted anything so spicy."

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Did you ever been there?  ✓ Have you ever been there?
✗
I haven't never seen it.  ✓ I've never seen it.
✗
Have you ever go to Rome?  ✓ Have you ever been to Rome?

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
been vs gone: "I've been to Rome" = I went and came back. "She's gone to Rome" = she's there now.
⭐
Use ever only in questions; drop it in positive answers: "Yes, I have."
🗣️
Follow-up details usually switch to the past simple: "Have you been to Rome?" — "Yes, I went last year."

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
Have you ever…? asks about experiences in your whole life.

Have+ subj +ever+participle ?
  • ever = at any time (questions); never = at no time.
  • Don't double the negative with never.
  • Details of when → past simple.

ru.title: Have you ever…? — жизненный опыт
ru.html:
Мы используем Present Perfect, чтобы спрашивать и говорить о жизненном опыте — о том, что происходило (или не происходило) когда-либо в вашей жизни. Точная дата не важна.

Два ключевых слова: в вопросах ever (= когда-либо) и never (= никогда) для отрицания.

🎯
"Have you ever been to Paris?" — "No, I've never been."

kk.title: Have you ever…? — өмірлік тәжірибе
kk.html:
Біз өмірлік тәжірибе туралы сұрап, айту үшін Present Perfect қолданамыз — бұл өміріңнің кез келген сәтінде болған (немесе болмаған) нәрселер. Нақты күні маңызды емес.

Екі кілт сөз: сұрақтарда ever (= кез келген уақытта), болымсыз түрде never (= еш уақытта).

🎯
"Have you ever been to Paris?" — "No, I've never been."

ru.title: Когда это используем?
ru.html:
🌏 Спросить об опыте
Have you ever eaten sushi?
🚫 Нет опыта
I've never ridden a horse.
🥇 Превосходная степень + ever
It's the best film I've ever seen.

Мы смотрим на всю вашу жизнь вплоть до настоящего момента — без конкретного времени.

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
🌏 Тәжірибе туралы сұра
Have you ever eaten sushi?
🚫 Тәжірибе жоқ
I've never ridden a horse.
🥇 Асырмалы шырай + ever
It's the best film I've ever seen.

Біз қазірге дейінгі бүкіл өміріңе қараймыз — нақты уақыт жоқ.

ru.title: Структура — ever & never
ru.html:
Have / Has+подлежащее+ever+причастие (V3) ?
?Have you ever been abroad?
+Yes, I've been to Spain.
−No, I've never been abroad.
⭐
never уже отрицательное — не добавляйте "not": говорите "I've never been", а не "I haven't never been".

kk.title: Құрылымы — ever & never
kk.html:
Have / Has+бастауыш+ever+өткен шақ есімшесі ?
?Have you ever been abroad?
+Yes, I've been to Spain.
−No, I've never been abroad.
⭐
never — өзі болымсыз сөз, сондықтан "not" қоспа: "I've never been" деп айт, "I haven't never been" деме.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Have you ever tried diving?
No, I've never done it. Have you?
Yes! It's the most amazing thing I've ever done.
💼 Собеседование
"Have you ever managed a team?"
🍜 Еда
"I've never tasted anything so spicy."

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Have you ever tried diving?
No, I've never done it. Have you?
Yes! It's the most amazing thing I've ever done.
💼 Сұхбат
"Have you ever managed a team?"
🍜 Тағам
"I've never tasted anything so spicy."

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
Did you ever been there?  ✓ Have you ever been there?
✗
I haven't never seen it.  ✓ I've never seen it.
✗
Have you ever go to Rome?  ✓ Have you ever been to Rome?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
Did you ever been there?  ✓ Have you ever been there?
✗
I haven't never seen it.  ✓ I've never seen it.
✗
Have you ever go to Rome?  ✓ Have you ever been to Rome?

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
been или gone: "I've been to Rome" = я съездил и вернулся. "She's gone to Rome" = она сейчас там.
⭐
Используйте ever только в вопросах; в утвердительных ответах опускайте его: "Yes, I have."
🗣️
Уточняющие детали обычно переходят в Past Simple: "Have you been to Rome?" — "Yes, I went last year."

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
been vs gone: "I've been to Rome" = бардым да қайттым. "She's gone to Rome" = ол қазір сонда.
⭐
Сұрақтарда ғана ever қолдан; болымды жауаптарда алып таста: "Yes, I have."
🗣️
Толықтырушы мәліметтер әдетте Past Simple-ке ауысады: "Have you been to Rome?" — "Yes, I went last year."

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
Have you ever…? спрашивает об опыте за всю вашу жизнь.

Have+ подлеж. +ever+причастие ?
  • ever = когда-либо (в вопросах); never = никогда.
  • Не удваивайте отрицание со словом never.
  • Детали когда → Past Simple.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
Have you ever…? бүкіл өміріңдегі тәжірибе туралы сұрайды.

Have+ бастауыш +ever+есімше ?
  • ever = кез келген уақытта (сұрақтарда); never = еш уақытта.
  • never сөзімен қос болымсыздық жасама.
  • when туралы мәлімет → Past Simple.

items.s: Have you ever been to Rome?
Did you ever been to Rome?

activities.why: Have + you + ever + participle; never is already negative.
Have + subject + ever + participle: “Have you ever been abroad?”

activities.after: ridden a horse — never in my life.

activities.instruction: Write one thing you have never done.

options.t: No, I've never done it. Have you?
No, I didn't never do it.
options.why: “I've never done it.” — don't double the negative.

activities.why: You asked and answered about experiences.

learn.title: How long have you…?
learn.html:
We use the present perfect for a situation that started in the past and is still true now — and to ask how long it has continued.

Think of your job, your home, a friendship: they began before and continue up to this moment.

🎯
"I have lived here for five years." → started 5 years ago, still living here now.

learn.title: When do we use it?
learn.html:
🏠 A state continuing now
We've known each other for years.
💼 Ongoing situation
She's had that car since 2020.
❓ Asking the duration
How long have you worked here?
5 yrs ago
still true
NOW

learn.title: Structure — how long + present perfect
learn.html:
How long+have / has+subject+past participle ?
?How long have you lived here?
answerI've lived here for ten years.
answerI've lived here since 2015.

English uses the present perfect here — many languages use the present, so watch this carefully.

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
How long have you known Sara?
We've been friends since school — about fifteen years.
Wow! And how long have you lived here?
I've lived here for three years.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
How long do you live here?  ✓ How long have you lived here?
✗
I live here since 2015.  ✓ I've lived here since 2015.
✗
I know her since years.  ✓ I've known her for years.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
Still true now? Then use the present perfect, not the present simple, for "how long".
⭐
Answer with for + a length of time or since + a starting point (next lesson!).
🗣️
Very common opener: "How long have you been…?" — used for jobs, cities, hobbies and relationships.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
How long have you…? asks about a situation still true now.

How long+have/has+participle ?
  • Started in the past + continues now → present perfect.
  • Answer with for (duration) or since (start point).
  • Not the present simple: say "have lived", not "live".

ru.title: How long have you…?
ru.html:
Мы используем Present Perfect для ситуации, которая началась в прошлом и всё ещё верна сейчас, — и чтобы спросить, как долго она длится.

Подумайте о работе, доме, дружбе: они начались раньше и продолжаются вплоть до этого момента.

🎯
"I have lived here for five years." → началось 5 лет назад, всё ещё живу здесь сейчас.

kk.title: How long have you…?
kk.html:
Present Perfect-ті өткенде басталып, әлі күнге дейін жалғасып жатқан жағдай үшін қолданамыз — және оның қанша уақыт созылғанын сұрау үшін.

Жұмысыңды, үйіңді, достықты ойла: олар бұрын басталып, осы сәтке дейін жалғасып келеді.

🎯
"I have lived here for five years." → 5 жыл бұрын басталды, әлі қазір де осында тұрамын.

ru.title: Когда это используем?
ru.html:
🏠 Состояние, длящееся сейчас
We've known each other for years.
💼 Текущая ситуация
She's had that car since 2020.
❓ Спрашиваем о длительности
How long have you worked here?
5 лет назад
всё ещё так
СЕЙЧАС

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
🏠 Қазір жалғасып жатқан күй
We've known each other for years.
💼 Жалғасып жатқан жағдай
She's had that car since 2020.
❓ Ұзақтығын сұрау
How long have you worked here?
5 жыл бұрын
әлі де солай
ҚАЗІР

ru.title: Структура — how long + Present Perfect
ru.html:
How long+have / has+подлежащее+причастие (V3) ?
?How long have you lived here?
ответI've lived here for ten years.
ответI've lived here since 2015.

Здесь английский использует Present Perfect — во многих языках употребляют настоящее время, так что будьте внимательны.

kk.title: Құрылымы — how long + Present Perfect
kk.html:
How long+have / has+бастауыш+өткен шақ есімшесі ?
?How long have you lived here?
жауапI've lived here for ten years.
жауапI've lived here since 2015.

Мұнда ағылшын тілі Present Perfect қолданады — көп тілде осы шақ қолданылады, сондықтан бұған мұқият бол.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
How long have you known Sara?
We've been friends since school — about fifteen years.
Wow! And how long have you lived here?
I've lived here for three years.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
How long have you known Sara?
We've been friends since school — about fifteen years.
Wow! And how long have you lived here?
I've lived here for three years.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
How long do you live here?  ✓ How long have you lived here?
✗
I live here since 2015.  ✓ I've lived here since 2015.
✗
I know her since years.  ✓ I've known her for years.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
How long do you live here?  ✓ How long have you lived here?
✗
I live here since 2015.  ✓ I've lived here since 2015.
✗
I know her since years.  ✓ I've known her for years.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Всё ещё верно сейчас? Тогда для "how long" используйте Present Perfect, а не Present Simple.
⭐
Отвечайте for + отрезок времени или since + начальная точка (следующий урок!).
🗣️
Очень частое начало: "How long have you been…?" — о работе, городах, хобби и отношениях.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Әлі де солай ма? Онда "how long" үшін Present Perfect қолдан, Present Simple емес.
⭐
Жауапты for + уақыт ұзақтығымен немесе since + бастау нүктесімен бер (келесі сабақ!).
🗣️
Өте жиі кездесетін бастама: "How long have you been…?" — жұмыс, қала, хобби және қарым-қатынас туралы қолданылады.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
How long have you…? спрашивает о ситуации, всё ещё верной сейчас.

How long+have/has+причастие ?
  • Началось в прошлом + длится сейчас → Present Perfect.
  • Отвечайте for (длительность) или since (начальная точка).
  • Не Present Simple: говорите "have lived", а не "live".

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
How long have you…? әлі де солай болып тұрған жағдай туралы сұрайды.

How long+have/has+есімше ?
  • Өткенде басталды + қазір жалғасады → Present Perfect.
  • Жауапты for (ұзақтық) немесе since (бастау нүктесі) арқылы бер.
  • Present Simple емес: "live" емес, "have lived" деп айт.

items.s: How long have you lived here?
How long do you live here?

activities.why: For a situation still true now, use the present perfect (have + participle).
still true now → present perfect: “I have lived here since 2015.”

why: present simple doesn't fit “how long… now”.
past simple is for finished time.

activities.before: “How long have you been here?” — “I've been here

activities.answer: how long have you studied english
alts: how long have you studied english?

activities.instruction: Write how long you have lived in your town (for/since).
activities.why: present perfect + for (length) or since (start point).

activities.scene: You meet someone new in your city.

steps.npc: How long have you lived here?

options.t: I've lived here for three years.
I live here since three years.

activities.why: You used the present perfect for a situation still true.

learn.title: for, since & ago
learn.html:
These three words measure time — and each has its own job.

for + a length of time · since + a starting point · ago = a time counted back from now.

🎯
for five years · since 2019 · five years ago.

learn.title: When do we use each?
learn.html:
for + periodsince + point in time
for two hourssince 9 o'clock
for a weeksince Monday
for ten yearssince 2015 / since I was a child
⭐
ago is different: it goes with the past simple — "I arrived two hours ago."

learn.title: Structure — which tense with which word
learn.html:
for + present perfectI've worked here for a year.
since + present perfectI've worked here since January.
ago + past simpleI started a year ago.

for and since answer "how long?" (still true now). ago answers "when?" (a finished point).

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
How long have you been here?
Since Monday — so for about four days.
And when did you land?
Four days ago.
🕰️ Duration
"We've waited for an hour."
📅 Start
"I've been ill since Friday."

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I've lived here since three years.  ✓ …for three years. (a length → for)
✗
I've worked here for 2019.  ✓ …since 2019. (a point → since)
✗
I have arrived two hours ago.  ✓ I arrived two hours ago. (ago → past simple)

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
for = a duration (how long). since = a start (from when).
⭐
ago counts backwards from now and always pairs with the past simple, never the present perfect.
🗣️
Quick test: can you put "the last…" in front? "for the last two years" ✓ — that's a for phrase.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• for + a period → present perfect (for a week).
  • since + a start point → present perfect (since Monday).
  • ago + past simple → a finished point (a week ago).

ru.title: for, since & ago
ru.html:
Эти три слова измеряют время — и у каждого своя роль.

for + отрезок времени · since + начальная точка · ago = время, отсчитанное назад от настоящего.

🎯
for five years · since 2019 · five years ago.

kk.title: for, since & ago
kk.html:
Бұл үш сөз уақытты өлшейді — әрқайсысының өз қызметі бар.

for + уақыт ұзақтығы · since + бастау нүктесі · ago = қазірден кері саналатын уақыт.

🎯
for five years · since 2019 · five years ago.

ru.title: Когда используем каждую форму?
ru.html:
for + периодsince + точка во времени
for two hourssince 9 o'clock
for a weeksince Monday
for ten yearssince 2015 / since I was a child
⭐
ago — другое: оно идёт с Past Simple — "I arrived two hours ago."

kk.title: Әрқайсысын қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
for + кезеңsince + уақыт нүктесі
for two hourssince 9 o'clock
for a weeksince Monday
for ten yearssince 2015 / since I was a child
⭐
ago басқаша: ол Past Simple-мен қолданылады — "I arrived two hours ago."

ru.title: Структура — какое время с каким словом
ru.html:
for + Present PerfectI've worked here for a year.
since + Present PerfectI've worked here since January.
ago + Past SimpleI started a year ago.

for и since отвечают на "how long?" (всё ещё верно сейчас). ago отвечает на "when?" (завершённый момент).

kk.title: Құрылымы — қай сөзбен қай шақ
kk.html:
for + Present PerfectI've worked here for a year.
since + Present PerfectI've worked here since January.
ago + Past SimpleI started a year ago.

for мен since "how long?" (әлі де солай) сұрағына жауап береді. ago "when?" (аяқталған сәт) сұрағына жауап береді.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
How long have you been here?
Since Monday — so for about four days.
And when did you land?
Four days ago.
🕰️ Длительность
"We've waited for an hour."
📅 Начало
"I've been ill since Friday."

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
How long have you been here?
Since Monday — so for about four days.
And when did you land?
Four days ago.
🕰️ Ұзақтық
"We've waited for an hour."
📅 Бастау
"I've been ill since Friday."

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
I've lived here since three years.  ✓ …for three years. (отрезок → for)
✗
I've worked here for 2019.  ✓ …since 2019. (точка → since)
✗
I have arrived two hours ago.  ✓ I arrived two hours ago. (ago → Past Simple)

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
I've lived here since three years.  ✓ …for three years. (ұзақтық → for)
✗
I've worked here for 2019.  ✓ …since 2019. (нүкте → since)
✗
I have arrived two hours ago.  ✓ I arrived two hours ago. (ago → Past Simple)

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
for = длительность (как долго). since = начало (с какого момента).
⭐
ago отсчитывает назад от настоящего и всегда сочетается с Past Simple, никогда с Present Perfect.
🗣️
Быстрая проверка: можно ли поставить впереди "the last…"? "for the last two years" ✓ — это фраза с for.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
for = ұзақтық (қанша уақыт). since = бастау (қашаннан).
⭐
ago қазірден кері санайды және әрқашан Past Simple-мен жұптасады, ешқашан Present Perfect-пен емес.
🗣️
Жылдам тексеру: алдына "the last…" қоюға бола ма? "for the last two years" ✓ — бұл for тіркесі.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• for + период → Present Perfect (for a week).
  • since + начальная точка → Present Perfect (since Monday).
  • ago + Past Simple → завершённый момент (a week ago).

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• for + кезең → Present Perfect (for a week).
  • since + бастау нүктесі → Present Perfect (since Monday).
  • ago + Past Simple → аяқталған сәт (a week ago).

activities.why: for + a length of time · since + a starting point.
a length of time → for: “…for three years.”

why: Correct — counting back from now → ago (with the past simple).
since needs the present perfect, not “started”.
Correct — a point with the past simple → in 2020.

activities.why: ago takes the past simple: “I arrived two hours ago.”

steps.npc: How long have you been in this city?

options.t: Since March — so for about four months.
For March.
