anyanybodyanythinganywhere
nonobodynothingnowhere
everyeverybodyeverythingeverywhere

kk.title: Әрқайсысын қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
адамдарзаттарорындар
somesomebodysomethingsomewhere
anyanybodyanythinganywhere
nonobodynothingnowhere
everyeverybodyeverythingeverywhere

ru.title: Структура — та же логика some/any
ru.html:
+ утвердительноеI saw someone.
− отрицательноеI didn't see anyone.
? вопросIs anyone there?
предложениеWould you like something to eat?
⭐
Эти слова берут глагол в единственном числе: "Everybody is ready."

kk.title: Құрылымы — сол some/any логикасы
kk.html:
+ болымдыI saw someone.
− болымсызI didn't see anyone.
? сұрақIs anyone there?
ұсынысWould you like something to eat?
⭐
Бұл сөздер жекеше етістік алады: "Everybody is ready."

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Is anyone home?
No, everybody's out. But I heard something.
Let's look everywhere.
There's nothing here.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Is anyone home?
No, everybody's out. But I heard something.
Let's look everywhere.
There's nothing here.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
I didn't see someone.  ✓ I didn't see anyone.
✗
Everybody are here.  ✓ Everybody is here.
✗
I don't go nowhere.  ✓ I don't go anywhere.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
I didn't see someone.  ✓ I didn't see anyone.
✗
Everybody are here.  ✓ Everybody is here.
✗
I don't go nowhere.  ✓ I don't go anywhere.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Они подчиняются тому же правилу: some- в утвердительных/предложениях, any- в отрицаниях/вопросах.
⭐
Все они единственного числа — используйте "is/was/does".
🗣️
-body и -one взаимозаменяемы (somebody = someone).

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Олар сол ережеге бағынады: some- болымды сөйлемдер мен ұсыныстарда, any- болымсыз сөйлемдер мен сұрақтарда.
⭐
Олардың бәрі жекеше — "is/was/does" қолдан.
🗣️
-body мен -one бір-бірін алмастыра алады (somebody = someone).

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• some/any/no/every + -body/-thing/-where.
  • Те же правила some/any; глагол в единственном числе.
  • -body = -one.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• some/any/no/every + -body/-thing/-where.
  • Сол some/any ережелері; жекеше етістік.
  • -body = -one.

activities.prompt: Match the pattern to a word.

activities.instruction: Offer politely: Would you like ___ to eat?

options.t: No, everybody's out. But I heard something.
No, everybody are out. But I heard anything.

activities.why: You used the compound words correctly.

learn.title: every & all
learn.html:
every and all both mean "the complete group", but the grammar differs. every + singular noun; all + plural or uncountable.

🎯
every student (singular) = all students (plural). Same idea, different form.

learn.title: When do we use each?
learn.html:
every + singularall + plural/uncountable
every dayall days → all the time
every child is…all children are…
every one of themall of them

learn.title: Structure
learn.html:
every+singular noun (+ singular verb)
all+plural / uncountable noun
everyEvery room is clean.
allAll the rooms are clean.
all + uncountableAll the water is gone.

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Do you exercise?
Yes, every morning. All my friends do too.
Did everyone come?
Every single person! All of them.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
every students  ✓ every student / all students.
✗
Every rooms are clean.  ✓ Every room is clean.
✗
all day → I work all the day  ✓ I work all day.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
every + singular noun + singular verb; all + plural noun + plural verb.
⭐
everybody/everything are singular even though they mean many.
🗣️
Time phrases: every day (each day) vs all day (the whole day).

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• every + singular noun (+ singular verb).
  • all + plural/uncountable.
  • everybody/everything are singular.

ru.title: every & all
ru.html:
every и all оба означают "вся группа целиком", но грамматика различается. every + единственное число существительного; all + множественное или неисчисляемое.

🎯
every student (единственное число) = all students (множественное). Одна идея, разная форма.

kk.title: every & all
kk.html:
every және all екеуі де "толық топ" дегенді білдіреді, бірақ грамматикасы бөлек. every + жекеше зат есім; all + көпше немесе саналмайтын зат есім.

🎯
every student (жекеше) = all students (көпше). Бір ой, әртүрлі форма.

ru.title: Когда используем каждую форму?
ru.html:
every + единственноеall + множественное/неисчисляемое
every dayall days → all the time
every child is…all children are…
every one of themall of them

kk.title: Әрқайсысын қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
every + жекешеall + көпше/саналмайтын
every dayall days → all the time
every child is…all children are…
every one of themall of them

ru.title: Структура
ru.html:
every+существительное в единственном числе (+ глагол в единственном числе)
all+множественное / неисчисляемое существительное
everyEvery room is clean.
allAll the rooms are clean.
all + неисчисляемоеAll the water is gone.

kk.title: Құрылымы
kk.html:
every+жекеше зат есім (+ жекеше етістік)
all+көпше / саналмайтын зат есім
everyEvery room is clean.
allAll the rooms are clean.
all + саналмайтынAll the water is gone.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Do you exercise?
Yes, every morning. All my friends do too.
Did everyone come?
Every single person! All of them.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Do you exercise?
Yes, every morning. All my friends do too.
Did everyone come?
Every single person! All of them.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
every students  ✓ every student / all students.
✗
Every rooms are clean.  ✓ Every room is clean.
✗
all day → I work all the day  ✓ I work all day.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
every students  ✓ every student / all students.
✗
Every rooms are clean.  ✓ Every room is clean.
✗
all day → I work all the day  ✓ I work all day.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
every + существительное в единственном числе + глагол в единственном числе; all + множественное существительное + глагол во множественном числе.
⭐
everybody/everything — единственного числа, хотя означают многих.
🗣️
Выражения времени: every day (каждый день) против all day (весь день).

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
every + жекеше зат есім + жекеше етістік; all + көпше зат есім + көпше етістік.
⭐
everybody/everything көпті білдірсе де, жекеше болып саналады.
🗣️
Уақыт тіркестері: every day (күн сайын) — all day (күні бойы).

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• every + существительное в единственном числе (+ глагол в единственном числе).
  • all + множественное/неисчисляемое.
  • everybody/everything — единственного числа.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• every + жекеше зат есім (+ жекеше етістік).
  • all + көпше/саналмайтын.
  • everybody/everything жекеше болып саналады.

activities.why: every + singular (+ singular verb); all + plural/uncountable.

activities.instruction: Rewrite with all: Every student passed. →

options.t: Every single person! All of them.
Every persons! All of it.

learn.title: all / most / some / no
learn.html:
These words describe how much of a group. From full to zero: all → most → some → no.

🎯
Used in general: "Most people like music." Used with a specific group: "most of the people here".

learn.title: When do we use of?
learn.html:
general (no "of")specific (of the / of + pronoun)
Most children like sweets.Most of the children here.
Some people agree.Some of us agree.
All cats sleep a lot.All of them slept.

learn.title: Structure — quantity + (of the) + noun
learn.html:
allall students / all of the students
mostmost days / most of my time
somesome books / some of these
nono idea / none of them
⭐
Add of before the / my / this / them: "most of the…", "some of them".

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Did the plan work?
Most of it did. Some of the steps failed, but no data was lost.
And the team?
All of them helped.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
most of people  ✓ most people / most of the people.
✗
all of students (general)  ✓ all students.
✗
some of us agrees  ✓ some of us agree.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
General group → no "of". A specific group (the/my/them) → add of.
⭐
Before a pronoun, always use of: all of us, some of them.
🗣️
Order of quantity: all > most > some > a few > no.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• all/most/some/no = amount of a group.
  • General → no "of"; specific → "of the / of them".
  • Before pronouns always use "of".

ru.title: all / most / some / no
ru.html:
Эти слова описывают, какую часть группы мы имеем в виду. От полного до нуля: all → most → some → no.

🎯
В общем смысле: "Most people like music." С конкретной группой: "most of the people here".

kk.title: all / most / some / no
kk.html:
Бұл сөздер топтың қаншасын сипаттайды. Толықтан нөлге дейін: all → most → some → no.

🎯
Жалпы мағынада: "Most people like music." Нақты топпен: "most of the people here".

ru.title: Когда используем of?
ru.html:
общий смысл (без "of")конкретный (of the / of + местоимение)
Most children like sweets.Most of the children here.
Some people agree.Some of us agree.
All cats sleep a lot.All of them slept.

kk.title: of-ты қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
жалпы ("of" жоқ)нақты (of the / of + есімдік)
Most children like sweets.Most of the children here.
Some people agree.Some of us agree.
All cats sleep a lot.All of them slept.

ru.title: Структура — количество + (of the) + существительное
ru.html:
allall students / all of the students
mostmost days / most of my time
somesome books / some of these
nono idea / none of them
⭐
Перед the / my / this / them добавляйте of: "most of the…", "some of them".

kk.title: Құрылымы — мөлшер + (of the) + зат есім
kk.html:
allall students / all of the students
mostmost days / most of my time
somesome books / some of these
nono idea / none of them
⭐
the / my / this / them алдында of қосыңыз: "most of the…", "some of them".

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Did the plan work?
Most of it did. Some of the steps failed, but no data was lost.
And the team?
All of them helped.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Did the plan work?
Most of it did. Some of the steps failed, but no data was lost.
And the team?
All of them helped.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
most of people  ✓ most people / most of the people.
✗
all of students (общий смысл)  ✓ all students.
✗
some of us agrees  ✓ some of us agree.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
most of people  ✓ most people / most of the people.
✗
all of students (жалпы)  ✓ all students.
✗
some of us agrees  ✓ some of us agree.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Общая группа → без "of". Конкретная группа (the/my/them) → добавьте of.
⭐
Перед местоимением всегда используйте of: all of us, some of them.
🗣️
Порядок по количеству: all > most > some > a few > no.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Жалпы топ → "of" жоқ. Нақты топ (the/my/them) → of қосыңыз.
⭐
Есімдік алдында әрқашан of қолданыңыз: all of us, some of them.
🗣️
Мөлшер реті: all > most > some > a few > no.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• all/most/some/no = количество группы.
  • Общий → без "of"; конкретный → "of the / of them".
  • Перед местоимениями всегда "of".

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• all/most/some/no = топтың мөлшері.
  • Жалпы → "of" жоқ; нақты → "of the / of them".
  • Есімдіктер алдында әрқашан "of".

items.s: Most of the people here agree.

activities.why: General → no “of”; specific (the/them) → “of the / of them”.

activities.after: children like sweets. (general — type – if none)

activities.instruction: Add of if needed: All ___ them came.

options.t: Most of it did, and all of the team helped.
Most of it did, and all of team helped.

learn.title: both / either / neither
learn.html:
These words talk about two things. both = the two together; either = one or the other; neither = not one and not the other.

🎯
both (2/2) · either (1 of 2) · neither (0 of 2).

learn.title: When do we use each?
learn.html:
✌️ both — the two
Both answers are right.
🤷 either — one of two
Either day is fine.
🚫 neither — not either
Neither option works.

learn.title: Structure — verb agreement
learn.html:
both + pluralBoth shops are open.
either/neither + singularEither bus goes there. Neither answer is right.
both … andBoth Tom and Sara came.
either … or / neither … norEither tea or coffee. Neither hot nor cold.

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Tea or coffee?
Either is fine, thanks.
Which restaurant did you like?
Neither, honestly. But both were cheap.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Both answer is right.  ✓ Both answers are right.
✗
Neither of them are here.  ✓ Neither of them is here. (formal: singular)
✗
either … nor  ✓ either … or; neither … nor.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
both = 2; either = 1 of 2; neither = 0 of 2.
⭐
Pairs: both…and, either…or, neither…nor.
🗣️
both + plural verb; either/neither + singular verb.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• both (2) + plural; either (1 of 2) & neither (0 of 2) + singular.
  • both…and · either…or · neither…nor.
  • Only for two items.

ru.title: both / either / neither
ru.html:
Эти слова говорят о двух вещах. both = обе вместе; either = одна или другая; neither = ни одна, ни другая.

🎯
both (2/2) · either (1 of 2) · neither (0 of 2).

kk.title: both / either / neither
kk.html:
Бұл сөздер екі зат туралы айтады. both = екеуі бірге; either = біреуі не екіншісі; neither = біреуі де, екіншісі де емес.

🎯
both (2/2) · either (1 of 2) · neither (0 of 2).

ru.title: Когда используем каждую форму?
ru.html:
✌️ both — оба
Both answers are right.
🤷 either — один из двух
Either day is fine.
🚫 neither — ни один
Neither option works.

kk.title: Әрқайсысын қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
✌️ both — екеуі
Both answers are right.
🤷 either — екеудің біреуі
Either day is fine.
🚫 neither — ешқайсысы емес
Neither option works.

ru.title: Структура — согласование глагола
ru.html:
both + множественное числоBoth shops are open.
either/neither + единственное числоEither bus goes there. Neither answer is right.
both … andBoth Tom and Sara came.
either … or / neither … norEither tea or coffee. Neither hot nor cold.

kk.title: Құрылымы — етістіктің үйлесуі
kk.html:
both + көпше түрBoth shops are open.
either/neither + жекеше түрEither bus goes there. Neither answer is right.
both … andBoth Tom and Sara came.
either … or / neither … norEither tea or coffee. Neither hot nor cold.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Tea or coffee?
Either is fine, thanks.
Which restaurant did you like?
Neither, honestly. But both were cheap.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Tea or coffee?
Either is fine, thanks.
Which restaurant did you like?
Neither, honestly. But both were cheap.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
Both answer is right.  ✓ Both answers are right.
✗
Neither of them are here.  ✓ Neither of them is here. (формально: единственное число)
✗
either … nor  ✓ either … or; neither … nor.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
Both answer is right.  ✓ Both answers are right.
✗
Neither of them are here.  ✓ Neither of them is here. (ресми: жекеше)
✗
either … nor  ✓ either … or; neither … nor.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
both = 2; either = 1 of 2; neither = 0 of 2.
⭐
Пары: both…and, either…or, neither…nor.
🗣️
both + глагол во множественном числе; either/neither + в единственном.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
both = 2; either = 1 of 2; neither = 0 of 2.
⭐
Жұптар: both…and, either…or, neither…nor.
🗣️
both + көпше етістік; either/neither + жекеше етістік.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• both (2) + мн. ч.; either (1 of 2) & neither (0 of 2) + ед. ч.
  • both…and · either…or · neither…nor.
  • Только для двух предметов.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• both (2) + көпше; either (1 of 2) & neither (0 of 2) + жекеше.
  • both…and · either…or · neither…nor.
  • Тек екі зат үшін.

activities.why: both + plural; either/neither + singular (they talk about two).

activities.after: is fine, thanks.” (one of the two)

why: Correct — not one and not the other → neither.

options.t: Either is fine, thanks.
Both is fine, thanks.
options.why: both + plural verb: “Both are fine” (or “Either is fine”).

learn.title: much / many / a lot
learn.html:
These express large quantity. many + countable; much + uncountable; a lot of works with both.

🎯
many books · much time · a lot of books/time.

learn.title: When do we use each?
learn.html:
many / mucha lot of
mostly in negatives & questionsmostly in positives
How many? / not mucha lot of friends
many (count) · much (uncount)a lot of (both)

learn.title: Structure
learn.html:
many + countableHow many people? Not many.
much + uncountableHow much money? Not much.
a lot of + botha lot of cars, a lot of water
⭐
In positive sentences, a lot of is more natural than much: "I have a lot of work" (not "much work").

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
How much time do we have?
Not much. And there are a lot of tasks left.
How many people are helping?
Not many, unfortunately.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
much books  ✓ many books.
✗
many money  ✓ much money / a lot of money.
✗
I have much friends.  ✓ I have a lot of friends.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
many = count; much = uncount; a lot of = both (and best for positives).
⭐
Questions & negatives love much/many; positives love a lot of.
🗣️
"too much / too many" and "so much / so many" follow the same count/uncount split.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• many + countable; much + uncountable.
  • a lot of = both, best in positives.
  • much/many favour questions & negatives.

ru.title: much / many / a lot
ru.html:
Они выражают большое количество. many + исчисляемое; much + неисчисляемое; a lot of подходит для обоих.

🎯
many books · much time · a lot of books/time.

kk.title: much / many / a lot
kk.html:
Бұлар көп мөлшерді білдіреді. many + саналатын зат есім; much + саналмайтын зат есім; a lot of екеуімен де қолданылады.

🎯
many books · much time · a lot of books/time.

ru.title: Когда используем каждую форму?
ru.html:
many / mucha lot of
в основном в отрицаниях и вопросахв основном в утверждениях
How many? / not mucha lot of friends
many (исч.) · much (неисч.)a lot of (оба)

kk.title: Әрқайсысын қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
many / mucha lot of
көбіне болымсыз және сұраулы сөйлемдердекөбіне болымды сөйлемдерде
How many? / not mucha lot of friends
many (саналатын) · much (саналмайтын)a lot of (екеуі де)

ru.title: Структура
ru.html:
many + исчисляемоеHow many people? Not many.
much + неисчисляемоеHow much money? Not much.
a lot of + обаa lot of cars, a lot of water
⭐
В утвердительных предложениях a lot of звучит естественнее, чем much: "I have a lot of work" (не "much work").

kk.title: Құрылымы
kk.html:
many + саналатынHow many people? Not many.
much + саналмайтынHow much money? Not much.
a lot of + екеуі деa lot of cars, a lot of water
⭐
Болымды сөйлемдерде a lot of much-қа қарағанда табиғирақ: "I have a lot of work" ("much work" емес).

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
How much time do we have?
Not much. And there are a lot of tasks left.
How many people are helping?
Not many, unfortunately.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
How much time do we have?
Not much. And there are a lot of tasks left.
How many people are helping?
Not many, unfortunately.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
much books  ✓ many books.
✗
many money  ✓ much money / a lot of money.
✗
I have much friends.  ✓ I have a lot of friends.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
much books  ✓ many books.
✗
many money  ✓ much money / a lot of money.
✗
I have much friends.  ✓ I have a lot of friends.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
many = исчисляемое; much = неисчисляемое; a lot of = оба (и лучше всего для утверждений).
⭐
Вопросы и отрицания любят much/many; утверждения любят a lot of.
🗣️
"too much / too many" и "so much / so many" следуют тому же делению исчисляемое/неисчисляемое.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
many = саналатын; much = саналмайтын; a lot of = екеуі де (болымды сөйлемдерге ең қолайлы).
⭐
Сұраулы және болымсыз сөйлемдер much/many-ды ұнатады; болымды сөйлемдер a lot of-ты ұнатады.
🗣️
"too much / too many" және "so much / so many" дәл сол саналатын/саналмайтын бөлінуін ұстанады.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• many + исчисляемое; much + неисчисляемое.
  • a lot of = оба, лучше всего в утверждениях.
  • much/many предпочитают вопросы и отрицания.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• many + саналатын; much + саналмайтын.
  • a lot of = екеуі де, болымды сөйлемдерге ең қолайлы.
  • much/many сұраулы және болымсыз сөйлемдерді қалайды.

activities.why: positive + countable → “a lot of friends” (or many).

activities.instruction: Complete a positive sentence: I have ___ (friends).
activities.why: positives prefer a lot of: “a lot of friends”.

steps.npc: How much time do we have?

options.t: Not much — and there are a lot of tasks left.
Not many — and there are much tasks left.
options.why: “Not much time… a lot of tasks”.

learn.title: (a) little / (a) few
learn.html:
These express small quantity. a few / a little = a small amount (positive). few / little (no "a") = almost none (negative feel).

🎯
"a few friends" = some. "few friends" = not many, sadly.

learn.title: When do we use each?
learn.html:
countable → (a) fewuncountable → (a) little
a few days (some)a little time (some)
few days (almost none)little time (almost none)

learn.title: Structure — the a makes it positive
learn.html:
a few + countableI have a few questions. (some)
few + countableFew people came. (not many)
a little + uncountableAdd a little salt. (some)
little + uncountableThere's little hope. (almost none)
⭐
With a = a positive "some"; without a = a negative "not enough".

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Do you have time?
A little. Give me a few minutes.
Many came to the event?
Sadly, few people showed up.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
a few water  ✓ a little water. (uncountable)
✗
a little books  ✓ a few books.
✗
I have few friends (meaning: some)  ✓ I have a few friends.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
few → countable; little → uncountable. The a turns it positive.
⭐
"a few / a little" = enough-ish; "few / little" = disappointingly not enough.
🗣️
"quite a few" actually means a fairly large number.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• a few/few + countable; a little/little + uncountable.
  • With a = positive (some); without = negative (not enough).

ru.title: (a) little / (a) few
ru.html:
Они выражают малое количество. a few / a little = немного (положительный смысл). few / little (без "a") = почти ничего (отрицательный оттенок).

🎯
"a few friends" = несколько. "few friends" = увы, немного.

kk.title: (a) little / (a) few
kk.html:
Бұлар аз мөлшерді білдіреді. a few / a little = аздап (болымды мағына). few / little ("a" жоқ) = дерлік жоқ (болымсыз реңк).

🎯
"a few friends" = бірнешеу. "few friends" = өкінішке қарай, көп емес.

ru.title: Когда используем каждую форму?
ru.html:
исчисляемое → (a) fewнеисчисляемое → (a) little
a few days (несколько)a little time (немного)
few days (почти нет)little time (почти нет)

kk.title: Әрқайсысын қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
саналатын → (a) fewсаналмайтын → (a) little
a few days (бірнешеу)a little time (аздап)
few days (дерлік жоқ)little time (дерлік жоқ)

ru.title: Структура — a делает смысл положительным
ru.html:
a few + исчисляемоеI have a few questions. (несколько)
few + исчисляемоеFew people came. (немного)
a little + неисчисляемоеAdd a little salt. (немного)
little + неисчисляемоеThere's little hope. (почти нет)
⭐
С a = положительное "немного"; без a = отрицательное "недостаточно".

kk.title: Құрылымы — a оны болымды етеді
kk.html:
a few + саналатынI have a few questions. (бірнешеу)
few + саналатынFew people came. (көп емес)
a little + саналмайтынAdd a little salt. (аздап)
little + саналмайтынThere's little hope. (дерлік жоқ)
⭐
a-мен = болымды "біраз"; a-сыз = болымсыз "жеткіліксіз".

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Do you have time?
A little. Give me a few minutes.
Many came to the event?
Sadly, few people showed up.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Do you have time?
A little. Give me a few minutes.
Many came to the event?
Sadly, few people showed up.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
a few water  ✓ a little water. (неисчисляемое)
✗
a little books  ✓ a few books.
✗
I have few friends (в значении: несколько)  ✓ I have a few friends.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
a few water  ✓ a little water. (саналмайтын)
✗
a little books  ✓ a few books.
✗
I have few friends (мағынасы: біраз)  ✓ I have a few friends.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
few → исчисляемое; little → неисчисляемое. a делает смысл положительным.
⭐
"a few / a little" = более-менее достаточно; "few / little" = увы, недостаточно.
🗣️
"quite a few" на самом деле означает довольно большое количество.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
few → саналатын; little → саналмайтын. a оны болымды етеді.
⭐
"a few / a little" = жеткілікті дерлік; "few / little" = өкінішке қарай жеткіліксіз.
🗣️
"quite a few" шын мәнінде едәуір көп санды білдіреді.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• a few/few + исчисляемое; a little/little + неисчисляемое.
  • С a = положительно (немного); без = отрицательно (недостаточно).

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• a few/few + саналатын; a little/little + саналмайтын.
  • a-мен = болымды (біраз); a-сыз = болымсыз (жеткіліксіз).

activities.why: a little + uncountable · a few + countable.

activities.q: “Sadly, ___ people came — the room was almost empty.”

steps.npc: Do you have time to help?

options.t: A little. Give me a few minutes.
A few. Give me a little minutes.

learn.title: Adjectives — describing things
learn.html:
Adjectives describe nouns — they tell us what someone or something is like (big, happy, red, interesting).

🎯
They go before a noun ("a big house") or after be ("The house is big").

learn.title: When do we use them?
learn.html:
📗 Before a noun
a beautiful garden
🔗 After be / seem / look
She looks tired.
🎨 Several adjectives
a nice old wooden table

learn.title: Structure — no plural, fixed order
learn.html:
adjective+noun
never pluraltwo red cars (not "reds")
after linking verbsIt tastes good. You seem happy.
orderopinion → size → age → colour → material

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
How was the hotel?
Lovely — a big, modern room with a beautiful view.
And the food?
It was delicious!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
a car red  ✓ a red car. (adjective first)
✗
two happys children  ✓ two happy children. (no plural)
✗
She looks happily.  ✓ She looks happy. (adjective after look)

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
Adjectives never change for plural: happy child, happy children.
⭐
After be, look, seem, feel, taste, sound use an adjective, not an adverb.
🗣️
Order feels natural with practice: "a nice big old blue box".

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• Adjectives describe nouns; before the noun or after be.
  • They never take a plural -s.
  • After linking verbs (look, seem) use an adjective.

ru.title: Прилагательные — описываем предметы
ru.html:
Прилагательные описывают существительные — они говорят, какой кто-то или что-то (big, happy, red, interesting).

🎯
Они стоят перед существительным ("a big house") или после be ("The house is big").

kk.title: Сын есімдер — заттарды сипаттау
kk.html:
Сын есімдер зат есімдерді сипаттайды — олар біреудің немесе бір нәрсенің қандай екенін айтады (big, happy, red, interesting).

🎯
Олар зат есімнің алдында ("a big house") немесе be-ден кейін ("The house is big") тұрады.

ru.title: Когда их используем?
ru.html:
📗 Перед существительным
a beautiful garden
🔗 После be / seem / look
She looks tired.
🎨 Несколько прилагательных
a nice old wooden table

kk.title: Оларды қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
📗 Зат есімнің алдында
a beautiful garden
🔗 be / seem / look-тан кейін
She looks tired.
🎨 Бірнеше сын есім
a nice old wooden table

ru.title: Структура — без множественного числа, фиксированный порядок
ru.html:
прилагательное+существительное
никогда не множественноеtwo red cars (not "reds")
после глаголов-связокIt tastes good. You seem happy.
порядокоценка → размер → возраст → цвет → материал

kk.title: Құрылымы — көпше түр жоқ, тұрақты рет
kk.html:
сын есім+зат есім
ешқашан көпше емесtwo red cars (not "reds")
байланыстырушы етістіктерден кейінIt tastes good. You seem happy.
ретпікір → көлем → жас → түс → материал

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
How was the hotel?
Lovely — a big, modern room with a beautiful view.
And the food?
It was delicious!

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
How was the hotel?
Lovely — a big, modern room with a beautiful view.
And the food?
It was delicious!

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
a car red  ✓ a red car. (сначала прилагательное)
✗
two happys children  ✓ two happy children. (без множественного)
✗
She looks happily.  ✓ She looks happy. (после look прилагательное)

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
a car red  ✓ a red car. (алдымен сын есім)
✗
two happys children  ✓ two happy children. (көпше түр жоқ)
✗
She looks happily.  ✓ She looks happy. (look-тан кейін сын есім)

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Прилагательные не меняются во множественном числе: happy child, happy children.
⭐
После be, look, seem, feel, taste, sound используйте прилагательное, а не наречие.
🗣️
Порядок ощущается естественно с практикой: "a nice big old blue box".

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Сын есімдер көпше түрде өзгермейді: happy child, happy children.
⭐
be, look, seem, feel, taste, sound-тан кейін үстеу емес, сын есім қолданыңыз.
🗣️
Рет тәжірибемен табиғи сезіледі: "a nice big old blue box".

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• Прилагательные описывают существительные; перед существительным или после be.
  • Они никогда не принимают окончание множественного числа -s.
  • После глаголов-связок (look, seem) используйте прилагательное.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• Сын есімдер зат есімдерді сипаттайды; зат есімнің алдында немесе be-ден кейін.
  • Олар ешқашан көпше -s жалғауын алмайды.
  • Байланыстырушы етістіктерден кейін (look, seem) сын есім қолданыңыз.

activities.why: Adjectives go before the noun and never take a plural -s.
opinion/size before the noun: “a big modern house”.

why: Correct — after taste use an adjective → good.

activities.instruction: Put in order: (old / a / table / wooden)
activities.why: age before material: “an old wooden table”.

activities.scene: A friend asks about your hotel.

options.t: Lovely — a big, modern room with a beautiful view.
Lovely — a room big and modern with a view beautiful.
options.why: “a big modern room… a beautiful view”.

learn.title: Adverbs — describing actions
learn.html:
Adverbs describe verbs (and adjectives/other adverbs) — they tell us how something happens. Many are made by adding -ly to an adjective.

🎯
quick → quickly: "She runs quickly."

learn.title: When do we use them?
learn.html:
🏃 How an action happens
He drives carefully.
🔆 Before an adjective
It's really good.
⭐ Irregular
good → well; fast → fast

learn.title: Structure — adjective + -ly
learn.html:
mostslow → slowly, quiet → quietly
-y → -ilyhappy → happily
irregulargood → well, fast → fast, hard → hard
adjective (describes noun)adverb (describes verb)
a careful driverdrives carefully
a good singersings well

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
How did the exam go?
I did well — I worked hard and answered carefully.
Great! You speak English fluently.
Thanks — I practise daily.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
She sings good.  ✓ She sings well.
✗
He drives careful.  ✓ He drives carefully.
✗
Run quicky / fastly.  ✓ Run quickly / fast.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
Adjective → noun; adverb → verb. "good/well" is the classic pair.
⭐
fast, hard, late, early are the same as adjective and adverb (no -ly).
🗣️
"hardly" is not the adverb of "hard" — it means "almost not". Be careful!

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• Adverbs describe verbs; often adjective + -ly.
  • Irregular: good→well, fast→fast, hard→hard.
  • Use an adverb (not adjective) with an action verb.

ru.title: Наречия — описываем действия
ru.html:
Наречия описывают глаголы (а также прилагательные/другие наречия) — они говорят, как что-то происходит. Многие образуются добавлением -ly к прилагательному.

🎯
quick → quickly: "She runs quickly."

kk.title: Үстеулер — әрекеттерді сипаттау
kk.html:
Үстеулер етістіктерді (және сын есімдер/басқа үстеулерді) сипаттайды — олар бір нәрсенің қалай болатынын айтады. Көбі сын есімге -ly қосу арқылы жасалады.

🎯
quick → quickly: "She runs quickly."

ru.title: Когда их используем?
ru.html:
🏃 Как происходит действие
He drives carefully.
🔆 Перед прилагательным
It's really good.
⭐ Неправильные
good → well; fast → fast

kk.title: Оларды қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
🏃 Әрекет қалай болатыны
He drives carefully.
🔆 Сын есімнің алдында
It's really good.
⭐ Дұрыс емес
good → well; fast → fast

ru.title: Структура — прилагательное + -ly
ru.html:
большинствоslow → slowly, quiet → quietly
-y → -ilyhappy → happily
неправильныеgood → well, fast → fast, hard → hard
прилагательное (описывает существительное)наречие (описывает глагол)
a careful driverdrives carefully
a good singersings well

kk.title: Құрылымы — сын есім + -ly
kk.html:
көбіslow → slowly, quiet → quietly
-y → -ilyhappy → happily
дұрыс емесgood → well, fast → fast, hard → hard
сын есім (зат есімді сипаттайды)үстеу (етістікті сипаттайды)
a careful driverdrives carefully
a good singersings well

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
How did the exam go?
I did well — I worked hard and answered carefully.
Great! You speak English fluently.
Thanks — I practise daily.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
How did the exam go?
I did well — I worked hard and answered carefully.
Great! You speak English fluently.
Thanks — I practise daily.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
She sings good.  ✓ She sings well.
✗
He drives careful.  ✓ He drives carefully.
✗
Run quicky / fastly.  ✓ Run quickly / fast.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
She sings good.  ✓ She sings well.
✗
He drives careful.  ✓ He drives carefully.
✗
Run quicky / fastly.  ✓ Run quickly / fast.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Прилагательное → существительное; наречие → глагол. "good/well" — классическая пара.
⭐
fast, hard, late, early одинаковы как прилагательное и наречие (без -ly).
🗣️
"hardly" — это не наречие от "hard", оно означает "почти не". Будьте внимательны!

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Сын есім → зат есім; үстеу → етістік. "good/well" — классикалық жұп.
⭐
fast, hard, late, early сын есім де, үстеу де ретінде бірдей (-ly жоқ).
🗣️
"hardly" — "hard"-тың үстеуі емес — ол "дерлік емес" дегенді білдіреді. Абай болыңыз!

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• Наречия описывают глаголы; часто прилагательное + -ly.
  • Неправильные: good→well, fast→fast, hard→hard.
  • С глаголом действия используйте наречие (не прилагательное).

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• Үстеулер етістіктерді сипаттайды; көбіне сын есім + -ly.
  • Дұрыс емес: good→well, fast→fast, hard→hard.
  • Әрекет етістігімен үстеу қолданыңыз (сын есім емес).

activities.prompt: Match each adjective to its adverb.
activities.why: Most add -ly; some are irregular (good→well, fast→fast).
the adverb of good is well: “He sings well.”

why: Correct — hard is both adjective and adverb.

activities.instruction: Complete: She's a good singer. → She sings ___

activities.scene: A friend asks how it went.

options.t: I did well — I worked hard and answered carefully.
I did good — I worked hardly and answered careful.

learn.title: Comparatives — comparing two things
learn.html:
Comparatives compare two things. Short adjectives add -er; long adjectives use more.

🎯
old → older; beautiful → more beautiful. Follow with than.

learn.title: When do we use each form?
learn.html:
short adj → -erlong adj → more
cheap → cheaperexpensive → more expensive
big → biggercareful → more careful
easy → easierinteresting → more interesting

learn.title: Structure — spelling & irregulars
learn.html:
1 syllable+ er: fast → faster
short vowel + cons.double: big → bigger
-y → -ierhappy → happier
2+ syllablesmore modern
irregulargood → better, bad → worse, far → further

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Is the new phone good?
It's faster and lighter, but more expensive.
Better than the old one?
Much better!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
more cheaper  ✓ cheaper. (don't use both)
✗
more good  ✓ better.
✗
expensiver  ✓ more expensive.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
Short adjective → -er; long adjective → more. Never both together.
⭐
Learn irregulars: good→better, bad→worse, far→further.
🗣️
Add much or a bit to show degree: "much better", "a bit cheaper".

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• Short adj + -er; long adj + more.
  • Irregular: better, worse, further.
  • Never combine "more" with "-er".

ru.title: Сравнительная степень — сравниваем два предмета
ru.html:
Сравнительная степень сравнивает два предмета. Короткие прилагательные добавляют -er; длинные используют more.

🎯
old → older; beautiful → more beautiful. Далее ставьте than.

kk.title: Салыстырмалы шырай — екі затты салыстыру
kk.html:
Салыстырмалы шырай екі затты салыстырады. Қысқа сын есімдер -er қосады; ұзын сын есімдер more қолданады.

🎯
old → older; beautiful → more beautiful. Соңынан than қойыңыз.

ru.title: Когда используем каждую форму?
ru.html:
короткое прил. → -erдлинное прил. → more
cheap → cheaperexpensive → more expensive
big → biggercareful → more careful
easy → easierinteresting → more interesting

kk.title: Әр форманы қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
қысқа сын есім → -erұзын сын есім → more
cheap → cheaperexpensive → more expensive
big → biggercareful → more careful
easy → easierinteresting → more interesting

ru.title: Структура — правописание и неправильные формы
ru.html:
1 слог+ er: fast → faster
краткий гласный + согл.double: big → bigger
-y → -ierhappy → happier
2+ слогаmore modern
неправильныеgood → better, bad → worse, far → further

kk.title: Құрылымы — емле және дұрыс емес түрлер
kk.html:
1 буын+ er: fast → faster
қысқа дауысты + дауыссызdouble: big → bigger
-y → -ierhappy → happier
2+ буынmore modern
дұрыс емесgood → better, bad → worse, far → further

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Is the new phone good?
It's faster and lighter, but more expensive.
Better than the old one?
Much better!

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Is the new phone good?
It's faster and lighter, but more expensive.
Better than the old one?
Much better!

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
more cheaper  ✓ cheaper. (не используйте оба)
✗
more good  ✓ better.
✗
expensiver  ✓ more expensive.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
more cheaper  ✓ cheaper. (екеуін бірге қолданбаңыз)
✗
more good  ✓ better.
✗
expensiver  ✓ more expensive.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Короткое прилагательное → -er; длинное → more. Никогда не вместе.
⭐
Выучите неправильные формы: good→better, bad→worse, far→further.
🗣️
Чтобы показать степень, добавьте much или a bit: "much better", "a bit cheaper".

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Қысқа сын есім → -er; ұзын сын есім → more. Ешқашан екеуін бірге қолданбаңыз.
⭐
Дұрыс емес түрлерді жаттаңыз: good→better, bad→worse, far→further.
🗣️
Дәрежені көрсету үшін much немесе a bit қосыңыз: "much better", "a bit cheaper".

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• Короткое прил. + -er; длинное + more.
  • Неправильные: better, worse, further.
  • Никогда не сочетайте "more" с "-er".

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• Қысқа сын есім + -er; ұзын сын есім + more.
  • Дұрыс емес: better, worse, further.
  • "more"-ды "-er"-мен ешқашан біріктірмеңіз.

activities.prompt: How do we make the comparative?

activities.instruction: Complete: My bag is ___ (heavy) than yours.

options.t: It's faster and lighter, but more expensive.
It's more faster and more light, but expensiver.

learn.title: …than… — completing comparisons
learn.html:
After a comparative, use than to introduce the second thing being compared.

🎯
"She's taller than me." · "This is better than that."

learn.title: When do we use it?
learn.html:
⚖️ Compare two
Gold is more expensive than silver.
👥 With pronouns
He's older than me / than I am.
📊 Degree words
much / a bit / far -er than

learn.title: Structure — comparative + than + noun/pronoun
learn.html:
comparative+than+noun / pronoun
+ nounwarmer than yesterday
+ object pronountaller than him (informal)
+ clausetaller than he is (formal)
degreemuch cheaper than…

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Is Almaty bigger than Astana?
Yes, and it's a bit warmer than the north.
Cheaper too?
Slightly cheaper than before.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
taller that me  ✓ taller than me.
✗
more big than  ✓ bigger than.
✗
as taller than  ✓ taller than / as tall as.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
Comparative sentences almost always need than for the second item.
⭐
"than me" (informal) and "than I am" (formal) are both correct.
🗣️
Don't confuse than (comparison) with then (time).

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• comparative + than + the second thing.
  • "than me" or "than I am".
  • than (compare) ≠ then (time).

ru.title: …than… — завершаем сравнение
ru.html:
После сравнительной степени используйте than, чтобы ввести второй сравниваемый предмет.

🎯
"She's taller than me." · "This is better than that."

kk.title: …than… — салыстыруды аяқтау
kk.html:
Салыстырмалы шырайдан кейін салыстырылатын екінші затты енгізу үшін than қолданыңыз.

🎯
"She's taller than me." · "This is better than that."

ru.title: Когда это используем?
ru.html:
⚖️ Сравниваем двоих
Gold is more expensive than silver.
👥 С местоимениями
He's older than me / than I am.
📊 Слова степени
much / a bit / far -er than

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
⚖️ Екеуін салыстыру
Gold is more expensive than silver.
👥 Есімдіктермен
He's older than me / than I am.
📊 Дәреже сөздері
much / a bit / far -er than

ru.title: Структура — сравнительная степень + than + существительное/местоимение
ru.html:
сравнительная степень+than+существительное / местоимение
+ существительноеwarmer than yesterday
+ местоимение-дополнениеtaller than him (неформально)
+ придаточноеtaller than he is (формально)
степеньmuch cheaper than…

kk.title: Құрылымы — салыстырмалы шырай + than + зат есім/есімдік
kk.html:
салыстырмалы шырай+than+зат есім / есімдік
+ зат есімwarmer than yesterday
+ толықтауыш есімдікtaller than him (бейресми)
+ бағыныңқы сөйлемtaller than he is (ресми)
дәрежеmuch cheaper than…

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Is Almaty bigger than Astana?
Yes, and it's a bit warmer than the north.
Cheaper too?
Slightly cheaper than before.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Is Almaty bigger than Astana?
Yes, and it's a bit warmer than the north.
Cheaper too?
Slightly cheaper than before.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
taller that me  ✓ taller than me.
✗
more big than  ✓ bigger than.
✗
as taller than  ✓ taller than / as tall as.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
taller that me  ✓ taller than me.
✗
more big than  ✓ bigger than.
✗
as taller than  ✓ taller than / as tall as.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Сравнительным предложениям почти всегда нужен than для второго предмета.
⭐
"than me" (неформально) и "than I am" (формально) — оба верны.
🗣️
Не путайте than (сравнение) с then (время).

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Салыстырмалы сөйлемдерге екінші зат үшін дерлік әрқашан than қажет.
⭐
"than me" (бейресми) және "than I am" (ресми) — екеуі де дұрыс.
🗣️
than-ды (салыстыру) then-мен (уақыт) шатастырмаңыз.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• сравнительная степень + than + второй предмет.
  • "than me" или "than I am".
  • than (сравнение) ≠ then (время).

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• салыстырмалы шырай + than + екінші зат.
  • "than me" немесе "than I am".
  • than (салыстыру) ≠ then (уақыт).

activities.why: comparative + than + the second thing (and bigger, not “more big”).

options.t: Yes, and it's a bit warmer than the north.
Yes, and it's a bit more warm that the north.

learn.title: not as … as — saying they're equal or not
learn.html:
as … as says two things are the same. not as … as says one is less than the other.

🎯
"as tall as" = equal · "not as tall as" = shorter.

learn.title: When do we use it?
learn.html:
🟰 Equal
She's as old as me.
➖ Less than
It's not as cold as yesterday.
📏 Same amount
as much / as many as

learn.title: Structure — as + adjective + as
learn.html:
(not) as+adjective+as
equalas good as the original
lessnot as expensive as I thought
= comparative"not as big as" = "smaller than"

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Is the sequel good?
It's not as good as the first film.
Is it as long?
Almost — nearly as long as the original.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
as tall than me  ✓ as tall as me.
✗
not so big than  ✓ not as big as.
✗
as more fast as  ✓ as fast as.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
as … as uses the base adjective (not comparative).
⭐
"not as … as" = a polite way to say "less …". "not as big as" = "smaller than".
🗣️
Common set phrases: "as soon as possible", "as much as you can".

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• as + adjective + as = equal.
  • not as … as = less than.
  • Use the base adjective, not the comparative.

ru.title: not as … as — говорим, что равны или нет
ru.html:
as … as говорит, что две вещи одинаковы. not as … as говорит, что одна меньше другой.

🎯
"as tall as" = равны · "not as tall as" = ниже.

kk.title: not as … as — тең немесе тең емес екенін айту
kk.html:
as … as екі нәрсенің бірдей екенін білдіреді. not as … as біреуінің екіншісінен кем екенін білдіреді.

🎯
"as tall as" = тең · "not as tall as" = аласарақ.

ru.title: Когда это используем?
ru.html:
🟰 Равны
She's as old as me.
➖ Меньше
It's not as cold as yesterday.
📏 Одинаковое количество
as much / as many as

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
🟰 Тең
She's as old as me.
➖ Кемірек
It's not as cold as yesterday.
📏 Бірдей мөлшер
as much / as many as

ru.title: Структура — as + прилагательное + as
ru.html:
(not) as+прилагательное+as
равноas good as the original
меньшеnot as expensive as I thought
= сравнительная степень"not as big as" = "smaller than"

kk.title: Құрылымы — as + сын есім + as
kk.html:
(not) as+сын есім+as
теңas good as the original
кемірекnot as expensive as I thought
= салыстырмалы шырай"not as big as" = "smaller than"

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Is the sequel good?
It's not as good as the first film.
Is it as long?
Almost — nearly as long as the original.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Is the sequel good?
It's not as good as the first film.
Is it as long?
Almost — nearly as long as the original.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
as tall than me  ✓ as tall as me.
✗
not so big than  ✓ not as big as.
✗
as more fast as  ✓ as fast as.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
as tall than me  ✓ as tall as me.
✗
not so big than  ✓ not as big as.
✗
as more fast as  ✓ as fast as.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
as … as использует начальную форму прилагательного (не сравнительную степень).
⭐
"not as … as" — вежливый способ сказать "less …". "not as big as" = "smaller than".
🗣️
Частые устойчивые фразы: "as soon as possible", "as much as you can".

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
as … as сын есімнің негізгі тұлғасын алады (салыстырмалы шырайды емес).
⭐
"not as … as" = "less …" дегенді сыпайы айтудың жолы. "not as big as" = "smaller than".
🗣️
Жиі кездесетін тұрақты тіркестер: "as soon as possible", "as much as you can".

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• as + adjective + as = равно.
  • not as … as = меньше.
  • Используй начальную форму прилагательного, а не сравнительную степень.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• as + adjective + as = тең.
  • not as … as = кемірек.
  • Салыстырмалы шырайды емес, сын есімнің негізгі тұлғасын қолдан.

items.s: It's not as cold as yesterday.

activities.q: “The sequel isn't as good as the first.” This means:
why: no — “not as good as” means less good.

activities.instruction: Rewrite: This film is worse than that one. → This film isn't ___
activities.why: worse than = not as good as.

options.t: It's not as good as the first film.
It's not as better as the first film.
options.why: use the base adjective: “as good as”.

learn.title: Superlatives — the most of all
learn.html:
Superlatives compare three or more and pick the top. Short adjectives add -est; long adjectives use the most. Always with the.

🎯
tall → the tallest; expensive → the most expensive.

learn.title: When do we use it?
learn.html:
🏆 The top of a group
the biggest city
🌍 With ever + present perfect
the best film I've ever seen
📍 in / of a group
the tallest in the class

learn.title: Structure — the + -est / the most
learn.html:
1 syllablethe fastest, the biggest
-y → -iestthe happiest
2+ syllablesthe most careful
irregularthe best, the worst, the furthest
⭐
Superlatives almost always take the in front.

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
What's the tallest mountain?
Everest — the highest in the world.
Best trip ever?
Japan — the most amazing place I've ever visited.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the most tallest  ✓ the tallest.
✗
the most good  ✓ the best.
✗
biggest city (no the)  ✓ the biggest city.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
Two things → comparative (-er/more). Three+ → superlative (the -est/most).
⭐
Use in for places/groups: "the best in the world/class".
🗣️
"the best … I've ever …" is a very common superlative pattern.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• Short adj → the -est; long adj → the most.
  • Always with the; irregular: the best/worst.
  • For 3+ things; "in the world/class".

ru.title: Superlatives — самый из всех
ru.html:
Superlatives (превосходная степень) сравнивают три и более и выбирают лучшее. Короткие прилагательные добавляют -est; длинные используют the most. Всегда с the.

🎯
tall → the tallest; expensive → the most expensive.

kk.title: Superlatives — бәрінің ең...
kk.html:
Superlatives (асырмалы шырай) үш немесе одан көп нәрсені салыстырып, ең үздігін таңдайды. Қысқа сын есімдерге -est жалғанады; ұзын сын есімдер the most қолданады. Әрдайым the артиклімен.

🎯
tall → the tallest; expensive → the most expensive.

ru.title: Когда это используем?
ru.html:
🏆 Лучший в группе
the biggest city
🌍 С ever + present perfect
the best film I've ever seen
📍 in / of — внутри группы
the tallest in the class

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
🏆 Топтың ең үздігі
the biggest city
🌍 ever + present perfect тіркесімен
the best film I've ever seen
📍 in / of — топ ішінде
the tallest in the class

ru.title: Структура — the + -est / the most
ru.html:
1 слогthe fastest, the biggest
-y → -iestthe happiest
2+ слогаthe most careful
неправильныеthe best, the worst, the furthest
⭐
Перед превосходной степенью почти всегда стоит the.

kk.title: Құрылымы — the + -est / the most
kk.html:
1 буынthe fastest, the biggest
-y → -iestthe happiest
2+ буынthe most careful
қалыптан тысthe best, the worst, the furthest
⭐
Асырмалы шырай алдында әрдайым дерлік the тұрады.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
What's the tallest mountain?
Everest — the highest in the world.
Best trip ever?
Japan — the most amazing place I've ever visited.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
What's the tallest mountain?
Everest — the highest in the world.
Best trip ever?
Japan — the most amazing place I've ever visited.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
the most tallest  ✓ the tallest.
✗
the most good  ✓ the best.
✗
biggest city (no the)  ✓ the biggest city.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
the most tallest  ✓ the tallest.
✗
the most good  ✓ the best.
✗
biggest city (no the)  ✓ the biggest city.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Две вещи → сравнительная степень (-er/more). Три и более → превосходная степень (the -est/most).
⭐
Для мест/групп используй in: "the best in the world/class".
🗣️
"the best … I've ever …" — очень частый образец превосходной степени.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Екі нәрсе → салыстырмалы шырай (-er/more). Үш және одан көп → асырмалы шырай (the -est/most).
⭐
Орын/топ үшін in қолдан: "the best in the world/class".
🗣️
"the best … I've ever …" — асырмалы шырайдың өте жиі кездесетін үлгісі.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• Короткое прил. → the -est; длинное прил. → the most.
  • Всегда с the; неправильные: the best/worst.
  • Для трёх и более вещей; "in the world/class".

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• Қысқа сын есім → the -est; ұзын сын есім → the most.
  • Әрдайым the артиклімен; қалыптан тыс: the best/worst.
  • Үш және одан көп нәрсе үшін; "in the world/class".

activities.prompt: How do we make the superlative?
activities.why: short adjectives → the -est; long adjectives → the most.

options.t: Japan — the most amazing place I've ever visited.
Japan — the amazingest place I ever visited.
options.why: “the most amazing place I've ever visited”.

learn.title: enough — the right amount
learn.html:
enough means "as much as needed". Its position depends on the word it goes with.

🎯
after adjectives/adverbs: "big enough". before nouns: "enough money".

learn.title: When do we use it?
learn.html:
📏 adjective + enough
It's not warm enough.
📦 enough + noun
We have enough time.
🎯 enough + to + verb
old enough to drive

learn.title: Structure — position matters
learn.html:
adj/adv + enoughstrong enough, quickly enough
enough + nounenough chairs, enough water
enough (+ for) + totall enough to reach; enough money to buy it
⭐
After an adjective/adverb, but before a noun.

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Can we afford it?
Not quite — we don't have enough money.
Is the box big enough?
Yes, just enough room.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
enough big  ✓ big enough. (after adjective)
✗
money enough  ✓ enough money. (before noun)
✗
old enough for drive  ✓ old enough to drive.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
adjective + enough + noun? No — enough goes after adjectives but before nouns.
⭐
Follow with to + verb or for + noun: "enough to eat", "enough for everyone".
🗣️
"That's enough!" is a common standalone phrase.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• after adjectives/adverbs (good enough).
  • before nouns (enough time).
  • + to + verb / for + noun.

ru.title: enough — нужное количество
ru.html:
enough означает "столько, сколько нужно". Его позиция зависит от слова, с которым оно стоит.

🎯
после прилагательных/наречий: "big enough". перед существительными: "enough money".

kk.title: enough — қажет мөлшер
kk.html:
enough "қажет мөлшерде" дегенді білдіреді. Оның орны қай сөзбен келгеніне байланысты өзгереді.

🎯
сын есім/үстеуден кейін: "big enough". зат есімнің алдында: "enough money".

ru.title: Когда это используем?
ru.html:
📏 adjective + enough
It's not warm enough.
📦 enough + noun
We have enough time.
🎯 enough + to + verb
old enough to drive

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
📏 adjective + enough
It's not warm enough.
📦 enough + noun
We have enough time.
🎯 enough + to + verb
old enough to drive

ru.title: Структура — позиция имеет значение
ru.html:
adj/adv + enoughstrong enough, quickly enough
enough + nounenough chairs, enough water
enough (+ for) + totall enough to reach; enough money to buy it
⭐
После прилагательного/наречия, но перед существительным.

kk.title: Құрылымы — орны маңызды
kk.html:
adj/adv + enoughstrong enough, quickly enough
enough + nounenough chairs, enough water
enough (+ for) + totall enough to reach; enough money to buy it
⭐
Сын есім/үстеуден кейін, бірақ зат есімнің алдында.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Can we afford it?
Not quite — we don't have enough money.
Is the box big enough?
Yes, just enough room.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Can we afford it?
Not quite — we don't have enough money.
Is the box big enough?
Yes, just enough room.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
enough big  ✓ big enough. (после прилагательного)
✗
money enough  ✓ enough money. (перед существительным)
✗
old enough for drive  ✓ old enough to drive.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
enough big  ✓ big enough. (сын есімнен кейін)
✗
money enough  ✓ enough money. (зат есімнің алдында)
✗
old enough for drive  ✓ old enough to drive.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
adjective + enough + noun? Нет — enough стоит после прилагательных, но перед существительными.
⭐
Дальше идёт to + verb или for + noun: "enough to eat", "enough for everyone".
🗣️
"That's enough!" — частая самостоятельная фраза.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
adjective + enough + noun? Жоқ — enough сын есімнен кейін, бірақ зат есімнің алдында тұрады.
⭐
Соңынан to + verb немесе for + noun жалғайды: "enough to eat", "enough for everyone".
🗣️
"That's enough!" — жиі қолданылатын дербес тіркес.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• после прилагательных/наречий (good enough).
  • перед существительными (enough time).
  • + to + verb / for + noun.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• сын есім/үстеуден кейін (good enough).
  • зат есімнің алдында (enough time).
  • + to + verb / for + noun.

options.t: Not quite — we don't have enough money.
Not quite — we don't have money enough.

learn.title: too — more than needed
learn.html:
too means "more than is good or necessary" — it has a negative feel. Don't confuse it with very (just "a lot").

🎯
"It's very hot" (a lot) vs "It's too hot" (a problem — I can't drink it).

learn.title: When do we use it?
learn.html:
🔥 too + adjective
It's too expensive.
📦 too much / too many
too much noise, too many people
🎯 too … to …
too tired to work

learn.title: Structure
learn.html:
too + adjectivetoo small, too slow
too much + uncountabletoo much sugar
too many + countabletoo many cars
too … to + verbtoo late to call
very (neutral)too (a problem)
very hot (a lot)too hot (can't handle it)

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Shall we buy it?
No, it's too expensive.
Tired?
Yes — too tired to cook. And there's too much to do.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
It's too good! (meaning: very good)  ✓ It's very good. (too = a problem)
✗
too many sugar  ✓ too much sugar.
✗
too tired for work  ✓ too tired to work.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
too = a problem (too much); very = just a strong "a lot".
⭐
too much + uncountable; too many + countable.
🗣️
"too … to …" shows a result: "too tired to think".

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• too = more than good (a problem), unlike very.
  • too much (uncount) / too many (count).
  • too … to + verb.

ru.title: too — больше, чем нужно
ru.html:
too означает "больше, чем хорошо или нужно" — у него негативный оттенок. Не путай с very (просто "очень").

🎯
"It's very hot" (очень) vs "It's too hot" (проблема — не могу выпить).

kk.title: too — қажеттен артық
kk.html:
too "жақсы немесе қажет болғаннан артық" дегенді білдіреді — оның жағымсыз реңкі бар. Оны very (жай ғана "өте") сөзімен шатастырма.

🎯
"It's very hot" (өте ыстық) vs "It's too hot" (мәселе — іше алмаймын).

ru.title: Когда это используем?
ru.html:
🔥 too + adjective
It's too expensive.
📦 too much / too many
too much noise, too many people
🎯 too … to …
too tired to work

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
🔥 too + adjective
It's too expensive.
📦 too much / too many
too much noise, too many people
🎯 too … to …
too tired to work

ru.title: Структура
ru.html:
too + adjectivetoo small, too slow
too much + неисчисляемоеtoo much sugar
too many + исчисляемоеtoo many cars
too … to + verbtoo late to call
very (нейтрально)too (проблема)
very hot (очень)too hot (невозможно вынести)

kk.title: Құрылымы
kk.html:
too + adjectivetoo small, too slow
too much + саналмайтынtoo much sugar
too many + саналатынtoo many cars
too … to + verbtoo late to call
very (бейтарап)too (мәселе)
very hot (өте ыстық)too hot (шыдай алмайсың)

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Shall we buy it?
No, it's too expensive.
Tired?
Yes — too tired to cook. And there's too much to do.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Shall we buy it?
No, it's too expensive.
Tired?
Yes — too tired to cook. And there's too much to do.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
It's too good! (meaning: very good)  ✓ It's very good. (too = проблема)
✗
too many sugar  ✓ too much sugar.
✗
too tired for work  ✓ too tired to work.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
It's too good! (meaning: very good)  ✓ It's very good. (too = мәселе)
✗
too many sugar  ✓ too much sugar.
✗
too tired for work  ✓ too tired to work.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
too = проблема (слишком много); very = просто сильное "очень".
⭐
too much + неисчисляемое; too many + исчисляемое.
🗣️
"too … to …" показывает результат: "too tired to think".

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
too = мәселе (қажеттен артық); very = жай ғана күшейтілген "өте".
⭐
too much + саналмайтын зат есім; too many + саналатын зат есім.
🗣️
"too … to …" нәтижені көрсетеді: "too tired to think".

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• too = больше, чем хорошо (проблема), в отличие от very.
  • too much (неисчисл.) / too many (исчисл.).
  • too … to + verb.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• too = жақсыдан артық (мәселе), very-ден өзгеше.
  • too much (саналмайтын) / too many (саналатын).
  • too … to + verb.

items.s: It's too hot to drink. (a problem)
This cake is very good. (a lot)
This cake is too good. (meaning: very good)

activities.why: too = more than is good (a problem); very = just “a lot”.

activities.q: “The film was ___ good — I loved it.”

options.t: No, it's too expensive.
options.why: too = a problem (more than we can spend).

options.t: No, it's very expensive to buy.

learn.title: Word order — verb + object
learn.html:
English word order is fairly fixed: subject + verb + object. The verb and its object usually stay together — nothing (like an adverb) comes between them.

🎯
"I speak English well." — not "I speak well English".

learn.title: When does this matter?
learn.html:
🔤 Basic order
I like coffee.
🚫 Keep verb + object together
I like coffee very much.
📍 Place before time
I went home early.

learn.title: Structure — S + V + O (+ place + time)
learn.html:
subject+verb+object+place+time
✓She read the book at home yesterday.
place before timeWe ate at the café last night.
⭐
Don't split the verb and object with an adverb: not "I like very much coffee".

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
What did you do?
I watched a film at home last night.
Did you enjoy it?
I liked it a lot.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I speak well English.  ✓ I speak English well.
✗
I like very much it.  ✓ I like it very much.
✗
We ate yesterday at home.  ✓ We ate at home yesterday. (place before time)

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
Keep verb + object together; adverbs go before or after, not between.
⭐
Order of extras: place before time (home yesterday).
🗣️
English rarely changes basic S-V-O order — it carries the meaning.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• Order: subject + verb + object.
  • Keep verb + object together.
  • Extras: place before time.

ru.title: Порядок слов — глагол + дополнение
ru.html:
Порядок слов в английском довольно фиксированный: подлежащее + глагол + дополнение. Глагол и его дополнение обычно стоят вместе — между ними ничего (например, наречия) не вставляется.

🎯
"I speak English well." — а не "I speak well English".

kk.title: Сөз тәртібі — етістік + толықтауыш
kk.html:
Ағылшын тіліндегі сөз тәртібі біршама тұрақты: бастауыш + етістік + толықтауыш. Етістік пен оның толықтауышы әдетте бірге тұрады — арасына ешнәрсе (мысалы, үстеу) кірмейді.

🎯
"I speak English well." — "I speak well English" емес.

ru.title: Когда это важно?
ru.html:
🔤 Базовый порядок
I like coffee.
🚫 Держи глагол + дополнение вместе
I like coffee very much.
📍 Место перед временем
I went home early.

kk.title: Бұл қашан маңызды?
kk.html:
🔤 Негізгі тәртіп
I like coffee.
🚫 Етістік + толықтауышты бірге ұста
I like coffee very much.
📍 Орын уақыттан бұрын
I went home early.

ru.title: Структура — S + V + O (+ место + время)
ru.html:
подлежащее+глагол+дополнение+место+время
✓She read the book at home yesterday.
место перед временемWe ate at the café last night.
⭐
Не разделяй глагол и дополнение наречием: не "I like very much coffee".

kk.title: Құрылымы — S + V + O (+ орын + уақыт)
kk.html:
бастауыш+етістік+толықтауыш+орын+уақыт
✓She read the book at home yesterday.
орын уақыттан бұрынWe ate at the café last night.
⭐
Етістік пен толықтауышты үстеумен бөлме: "I like very much coffee" емес.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
What did you do?
I watched a film at home last night.
Did you enjoy it?
I liked it a lot.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
What did you do?
I watched a film at home last night.
Did you enjoy it?
I liked it a lot.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
I speak well English.  ✓ I speak English well.
✗
I like very much it.  ✓ I like it very much.
✗
We ate yesterday at home.  ✓ We ate at home yesterday. (место перед временем)

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
I speak well English.  ✓ I speak English well.
✗
I like very much it.  ✓ I like it very much.
✗
We ate yesterday at home.  ✓ We ate at home yesterday. (орын уақыттан бұрын)

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Держи глагол + дополнение вместе; наречия идут до или после, но не между.
⭐
Порядок дополнительных членов: место перед временем (home yesterday).
🗣️
Английский редко меняет базовый порядок S-V-O — именно он несёт смысл.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Етістік + толықтауышты бірге ұста; үстеулер алдында немесе соңында тұрады, арасында емес.
⭐
Қосымша мүшелердің тәртібі: орын уақыттан бұрын (home yesterday).
🗣️
Ағылшын тілі негізгі S-V-O тәртібін сирек өзгертеді — мағына соған сүйенеді.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• Порядок: подлежащее + глагол + дополнение.
  • Держи глагол + дополнение вместе.
  • Дополнения: место перед временем.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• Тәртіп: бастауыш + етістік + толықтауыш.
  • Етістік + толықтауышты бірге ұста.
  • Қосымшалар: орын уақыттан бұрын.

activities.why: Keep the verb and object together; the adverb doesn't come between them.
keep verb + object together: “speak English well”.
place before time: “ate at home yesterday”.

options: I like very much this song.
I like this song very much.
I very much like this song too.
why: Correct — “I like this song very much.”

activities.instruction: Put in order: (home / went / early / I)

activities.scene: A friend asks about your evening.

options.t: I watched a film at home last night.
I watched at home a film last night.

learn.title: always / usually / often — adverbs of frequency
learn.html:
Adverbs of frequency say how often. They usually go before the main verb, but after "be".

🎯
"I always get up early." · "She is always late."

learn.title: When / how often?
learn.html:
📊 The scale
always → usually → often → sometimes → rarely → never
🔧 Before the main verb
I often walk to work.
🟦 After be
He is usually busy.

learn.title: Structure — position rules
learn.html:
before main verbThey always help.
after beShe is never rude.
with auxiliaryI have never been there.
sometimes/usuallycan also start a sentence: Sometimes I walk.
⭐
never is already negative — the verb stays positive: "I never eat meat."

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Do you exercise?
I usually run and I sometimes swim. I never skip Mondays.
Is the gym busy?
It's always full in the evening.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I go always early.  ✓ I always go early.
✗
She always is late.  ✓ She is always late.
✗
I don't never eat meat.  ✓ I never eat meat.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
Before the main verb, after "be". That's the core rule.
⭐
"How often…?" questions are answered with these adverbs (or "every day", "twice a week").
🗣️
Don't add another negative with never.

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• always→never scale of frequency.
  • Before the main verb, after be.
  • never = already negative.

ru.title: always / usually / often — наречия частоты
ru.html:
Наречия частоты говорят, как часто. Они обычно стоят перед основным глаголом, но после "be".

🎯
"I always get up early." · "She is always late."

kk.title: always / usually / often — жиілік үстеулері
kk.html:
Жиілік үстеулері әрекеттің қаншалықты жиі болатынын айтады. Олар әдетте негізгі етістіктің алдында тұрады, бірақ "be" етістігінен кейін.

🎯
"I always get up early." · "She is always late."

ru.title: Когда / как часто?
ru.html:
📊 Шкала
always → usually → often → sometimes → rarely → never
🔧 Перед основным глаголом
I often walk to work.
🟦 После be
He is usually busy.

kk.title: Қашан / қаншалықты жиі?
kk.html:
📊 Шкала
always → usually → often → sometimes → rarely → never
🔧 Негізгі етістіктің алдында
I often walk to work.
🟦 be-дан кейін
He is usually busy.

ru.title: Структура — правила позиции
ru.html:
перед основным глаголомThey always help.
после beShe is never rude.
со вспомогательным глаголомI have never been there.
sometimes/usuallyмогут и начинать предложение: Sometimes I walk.
⭐
never уже отрицательное — глагол остаётся в утвердительной форме: "I never eat meat."

kk.title: Құрылымы — орналасу ережелері
kk.html:
негізгі етістіктің алдындаThey always help.
be-дан кейінShe is never rude.
көмекші етістікпенI have never been there.
sometimes/usuallyсөйлемді бастай да алады: Sometimes I walk.
⭐
never өзі болымсыз — етістік болымды күйінде қалады: "I never eat meat."

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Do you exercise?
I usually run and I sometimes swim. I never skip Mondays.
Is the gym busy?
It's always full in the evening.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Do you exercise?
I usually run and I sometimes swim. I never skip Mondays.
Is the gym busy?
It's always full in the evening.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
I go always early.  ✓ I always go early.
✗
She always is late.  ✓ She is always late.
✗
I don't never eat meat.  ✓ I never eat meat.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
I go always early.  ✓ I always go early.
✗
She always is late.  ✓ She is always late.
✗
I don't never eat meat.  ✓ I never eat meat.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
Перед основным глаголом, после "be". Это и есть главное правило.
⭐
На вопросы "How often…?" отвечают этими наречиями (или "every day", "twice a week").
🗣️
Не добавляй второе отрицание с never.

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
Негізгі етістіктің алдында, "be"-дан кейін. Міне, негізгі ереже осы.
⭐
"How often…?" сұрақтарына осы үстеулермен (немесе "every day", "twice a week") жауап беріледі.
🗣️
never-ге тағы бір болымсыздық қоспа.

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• always→never шкала частоты.
  • Перед основным глаголом, после be.
  • never = уже отрицательное.

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• always→never жиілік шкаласы.
  • Негізгі етістіктің алдында, be-дан кейін.
  • never = өзі болымсыз.

activities.why: Frequency adverbs go before the main verb, but after be.
with I use the base verb: “I always eat breakfast.” (the adverb before the verb is fine)
before the main verb: “He always comes late.”
before the main verb: “I never eat meat.”

options.t: I usually run and I sometimes swim. I never skip Mondays.
I run usually and I swim sometimes. I skip never Mondays.

learn.title: still / yet / already
learn.html:
These adverbs talk about time and expectation. still = continuing · already = sooner than expected · yet = up to now (questions/negatives).

🎯
"He's still here." · "I've already eaten." · "Not yet."

learn.title: When do we use each?
learn.html:
⏳ still — not finished
She's still sleeping.
✅ already — done early
They've already left.
❓ yet — questions/negatives
Are you ready yet?

learn.title: Structure — position
learn.html:
stillbefore the main verb / after be: I still live here.
alreadymiddle position: I've already done it.
yetend of the sentence: Not finished yet.
still (positive continuing)yet (question/negative)
He's still working.He hasn't finished yet.

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Have you finished yet?
Not yet — I'm still working.
Should I start dinner?
I've already cooked!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Have you still finished?  ✓ Have you finished yet?
✗
I still didn't eat.  ✓ I still haven't eaten. / I haven't eaten yet.
✗
I've yet finished.  ✓ I've already finished.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
still = keeps going · already = done (surprisingly soon) · yet = up to now (?/−).
⭐
yet goes at the end; still/already go in the middle.
🗣️
"still" can also show surprise/impatience: "You're still not ready?"

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• still = continuing (middle position).
  • already = sooner than expected (middle).
  • yet = up to now, in questions/negatives (end).

ru.title: still / yet / already
ru.html:
Эти наречия говорят о времени и ожидании. still = всё ещё продолжается · already = раньше, чем ожидалось · yet = до сих пор (в вопросах/отрицаниях).

🎯
"He's still here." · "I've already eaten." · "Not yet."

kk.title: still / yet / already
kk.html:
Бұл үстеулер уақыт пен күтім туралы айтады. still = әлі жалғасып жатыр · already = күткеннен ерте · yet = осы кезге дейін (сұраулы/болымсыз сөйлемдерде).

🎯
"He's still here." · "I've already eaten." · "Not yet."

ru.title: Когда используем каждую форму?
ru.html:
⏳ still — не закончено
She's still sleeping.
✅ already — сделано рано
They've already left.
❓ yet — вопросы/отрицания
Are you ready yet?

kk.title: Әрқайсысын қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
⏳ still — әлі бітпеген
She's still sleeping.
✅ already — ерте бітті
They've already left.
❓ yet — сұраулы/болымсыз
Are you ready yet?

ru.title: Структура — позиция
ru.html:
stillперед основным глаголом / после be: I still live here.
alreadyв середине: I've already done it.
yetв конце предложения: Not finished yet.
still (утвердит., продолжается)yet (вопрос/отрицание)
He's still working.He hasn't finished yet.

kk.title: Құрылымы — орналасуы
kk.html:
stillнегізгі етістіктің алдында / be-дан кейін: I still live here.
alreadyорта тұста: I've already done it.
yetсөйлемнің соңында: Not finished yet.
still (болымды, жалғасып жатқан)yet (сұраулы/болымсыз)
He's still working.He hasn't finished yet.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Have you finished yet?
Not yet — I'm still working.
Should I start dinner?
I've already cooked!

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Have you finished yet?
Not yet — I'm still working.
Should I start dinner?
I've already cooked!

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html:
✗
Have you still finished?  ✓ Have you finished yet?
✗
I still didn't eat.  ✓ I still haven't eaten. / I haven't eaten yet.
✗
I've yet finished.  ✓ I've already finished.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html:
✗
Have you still finished?  ✓ Have you finished yet?
✗
I still didn't eat.  ✓ I still haven't eaten. / I haven't eaten yet.
✗
I've yet finished.  ✓ I've already finished.

ru.title: Полезные советы
ru.html:
🧠
still = продолжается · already = сделано (удивительно рано) · yet = до сих пор (?/−).
⭐
yet идёт в конце; still/already в середине.
🗣️
"still" может выражать удивление/нетерпение: "You're still not ready?"

kk.title: Пайдалы кеңестер
kk.html:
🧠
still = әлі жалғасады · already = бітті (таңқаларлықтай ерте) · yet = осы кезге дейін (?/−).
⭐
yet соңында тұрады; still/already орта тұста тұрады.
🗣️
"still" таңдану/шыдамсыздықты да білдіре алады: "You're still not ready?"

ru.title: Итог — запиши в тетрадь
ru.html:
• still = продолжается (в середине).
  • already = раньше, чем ожидалось (в середине).
  • yet = до сих пор, в вопросах/отрицаниях (в конце).

kk.title: Қорытынды — дәптерге жазып ал
kk.html:
• still = жалғасып жатыр (орта тұста).
  • already = күткеннен ерте (орта тұста).
  • yet = осы кезге дейін, сұраулы/болымсыз сөйлемдерде (соңында).

activities.why: still = continuing (middle) · already = middle · yet = end (questions/negatives).

options.t: Not yet — I'm still working.
Not still — I'm yet working.

learn.title: Give it to me! — two objects
learn.html:
Some verbs (give, send, bring, show, lend) take two objects: a person and a thing. There are two correct orders.

🎯
"Give me the book." OR "Give the book to me."

learn.title: When do we use each order?
learn.html:
verb + person + thingverb + thing + to/for + person
She gave me a gift.She gave a gift to me.
Send him the file.Send the file to him.
Buy her flowers.Buy flowers for her.

learn.title: Structure — watch the pronoun
learn.html:
person + thinggive me the pen
thing + to/for + persongive the pen to me
both pronounsGive it to me. (thing first, then "to")
⭐
When the thing is a pronoun (it/them), use "…it to me": "Give it to me," not "Give me it".

learn.title: Real-life examples
learn.html:
Can you send me the report?
Sure, I'll send it to you now.
And show Ana the photos?
I already showed them to her.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Give me it.  ✓ Give it to me.
✗
She gave to me a gift.  ✓ She gave me a gift / a gift to me.
✗
Buy to her flowers.  ✓ Buy her flowers / flowers for her.

learn.title: Practical tips
learn.html:
🧠
Two orders: person + thing OR thing + to/for + person.
⭐
Some verbs use to (give, send, show); some use for (buy, make, get).
🗣️
When the thing is "it/them", prefer "…it to me".

learn.title: Summary — for your notebook
learn.html:
• give/send/show + person + thing, or + thing + to + person.
  • buy/make/get use for.
  • Pronoun thing → "give it to me".

ru.title: Give it to me! — два дополнения
ru.html:
Некоторые глаголы (give, send, bring, show, lend) берут два дополнения: человека и вещь. Есть два правильных порядка.

🎯
"Give me the book." ИЛИ "Give the book to me."

kk.title: Give it to me! — екі толықтауыш
kk.html:
Кейбір етістіктер (give, send, bring, show, lend) екі толықтауыш алады: адам мен зат. Мұнда екі дұрыс тәртіп бар.

🎯
"Give me the book." НЕМЕСЕ "Give the book to me."

ru.title: Когда используем каждый порядок?
ru.html:
verb + person + thingverb + thing + to/for + person
She gave me a gift.She gave a gift to me.
Send him the file.Send the file to him.
Buy her flowers.Buy flowers for her.

kk.title: Әр тәртіпті қашан қолданамыз?
kk.html:
verb + person + thingverb + thing + to/for + person
She gave me a gift.She gave a gift to me.
Send him the file.Send the file to him.
Buy her flowers.Buy flowers for her.

ru.title: Структура — следи за местоимением
ru.html:
person + thinggive me the pen
thing + to/for + persongive the pen to me
оба местоименияGive it to me. (сначала вещь, потом "to")
⭐
Когда вещь — местоимение (it/them), используй "…it to me": "Give it to me," а не "Give me it".

kk.title: Құрылымы — есімдікке назар аудар
kk.html:
person + thinggive me the pen
thing + to/for + persongive the pen to me
екеуі де есімдікGive it to me. (алдымен зат, сосын "to")
⭐
Зат есімдік болғанда (it/them), "…it to me" қолдан: "Give it to me," "Give me it" емес.

ru.title: Примеры из жизни
ru.html:
Can you send me the report?
Sure, I'll send it to you now.
And show Ana the photos?
I already showed them to her.

kk.title: Нақты өмірден мысалдар
kk.html:
Can you send me the report?
